* A market-based salary, depending on your knowledge and experience ranging between €5,000 and €8,000 per month.
* Company-wide courses and individual training opportunities to further develop yourself
* More than 250 passionate colleagues to work with and learn from
* Fun Northwave events and parties, look here on our work page for an overview
* Several years (5+) of experience as a cyber security consultant, or in a similar role.
* Expertise in security management, risk management, relevant standards and legislation. But most importantly; we are looking for someone with a real passion for the profession. A ‘vakidioot’ as we call it within Northwave, with an ‘expert leadership’ influence on the team.
* An entrepreneurial mindset and no problem working independent.
* Proficiency in Swedish and English, both spoken and written.
* CISM/CISSP/CISA/CRISK/CIPM or related certifications are a plus, but not necessary.


**Interested?**
 What is important to you in your next step? We would love to hear from you! For more information, apply directly or contact our recruiter Youri Roelofs via youri.roelofs@northwave-cybersecurity.com

title: Microsoft Security Consultant
description:
**Summary**
 At Avanade, you will support our customers in understanding security risks and improving their data protection solutions. You’ll work across both Avanade and Accenture clients, ranging from medium to large global enterprises. You’ll be part of a collaborative and diverse team that values learning, knowledge sharing, and practical problem solving – helping you to grow confidence and capability as a security consultant. We look for consultants who are curious, proactive, and comfortable challenging ideas – while continuing to learn and grow their expertise.
   

  

 If you already are a passionate security consultant and want to take the next step in a consultant role where you can shape your future, broaden your impact and grow into more complex responsibilities – this is the opportunity for you.
   

  

 We believe you can be our next innovator, risk-taker, and challenger of the status quo at Avanade.
   

  

**Come join us**
 As a member of Avanade’s security practice, you will be in a unique position to work for both Avanade and Accenture clients, supporting security and data protection initiatives for mid-size organizations and large global enterprises. You will contribute directly to client engagements, working hands-on with Microsoft security technologies while collaborating closely with experienced consultants.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 As a member of Avanade’s security practice, you will be in a unique position to work for both Avanade and Accenture clients, supporting security and data protection initiatives for mid-size organizations and large global enterprises. You will contribute directly to client engagements, working hands-on with Microsoft security technologies while collaborating closely with experienced consultants.
   

  

 You’re a team-oriented security consultant with broad Microsoft 365 understanding especially in security and compliance products. You enjoy working with technologies like Purview, Entra, Defender and Sentinel, and hopefully some non-Microsoft tools. You like working with others, value different perspectives, and are comfortable asking questions to deepen your understanding and grow your skills.
   

  

**What You’ll Do In This Role**
* Design and deliver Microsoftbased security and data protection solutions
* Support customer workshops, assessments, and solution discussions, with expectations to lead as you grow in the role
* Take an active role in knowledge sharing, continuous improvement, and evolving how we deliver security services
* Stay current with Microsoft security technologies and partner solutions through training, certification, and handson project work
* Proactively suggest improvements, challenge approaches when appropriate, and take responsibility for your own development
* Take an active role at partner events and customer meetings


 This role is designed for consultants who already have practical experience and are eager to take the next step — gaining broader exposure, increase responsibility, and the opportunity to grow into more complex and impactful security engagements.
   

  

**Qualifications**
**Required Skills & Experience:**
* Proven experience with Microsoft Security technologies including Microsoft Purview, Defender, Sentinel, and Entra ID.
* Hands-on experience with DLP policy design, migration and refinement
* Strong understanding of data protection regulations (e.g. GDPR, DPA 2018, ISO 27001, NIST, EU AI Act).
* Familiarity with AI security concepts, including DSPM, model governance, and data lineage.
* Understanding of Zero Trust architecture and cloud-native security controls.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
* Relevant certifications (e.g., SC-400, SC-200, AZ-500, CISSP, CIPP/E, AI-specific certs) are highly desirable,


**Skills**
 Ideally, you would have had experience working on the Microsoft platform, however we can provide focused training to the right candidates who can demonstrate their passion, experience and thought leadership in this space. To hit the ground running, your skills may include:
   

  

* Proven experience of Microsoft Information Protection and with data classification infrastructures and techniques to classifying unstructured and structured data both on-premises and in the cloud.
* Proven experience of Data Loss Prevention technologies across endpoints, email and the internet, including Microsoft Purview.
* Proven experience securing Microsoft 365, leveraging Microsoft Secure Score with a deep understanding of the security capabilities of Microsoft 365.
* Proven experience in techniques and approaches towards the protection of data stored in databases and applications.
* Deep expertise in data confidentiality and data integrity principles.
* Deep expertise of business, privacy, security, and compliance challenges su-rounding Data Protection, including being able to research and understand data privacy requirements.
* A keen interest in Cyber Security, maintaining a good understanding of capabilities across the industry, and in developing their own Cyber experience to continue progression.
* With an eye to the future, you are aware of emerging technologies in the Data Protection space.


**Characteristics That Can Spell Success For This Role**
* Experience in consulting and advisory discussions with clients and other consulting firms.
* Experience in owning the design and implementation of technology solutions.
* You are a Subject matter expert, who has experience working on the Microsoft platform.
* Experience managing deliverables and being responsible for delivering against critical milestones.
* Ability to collaborate within a large global organization.
* You have implemented work plans for assigned projects, programs and clients against established operational objective.
* You are seen as a role model and act as a leader.


**Some Of The Best Things About Working At Avanade**
* Opportunity to work for Microsoft’s Global Alliance Partner of the Year


 As you bring your skills and abilities to Avanade, you’ll get distinctive experiences, limitless learning, and ambitious growth in return. As we continue to build our diverse and inclusive culture, we become even more innovative and creative, helping us better serve our clients and communities. You’ll join a community of smart, supportive collaborators to lift, mentor, and guide you, and to lean on your expertise. You get a company purpose-built for business-critical, leading-edge technology solutions, committed to improving the way humans work, interact, and live. It’s all here, so take a closer look!
   

  

**Together We Do What Matters.**
 We work hard to provide an inclusive, diverse culture with a deep sense of belonging for all our employees. Visit our Inclusion & Diversity page.
   

  

 Create a future for our people that focuses on
   

  

* Expanding your thinking
* Experimenting courageously
* Learning and pivoting


 Inspire greatness in our people by
   

  

* Empowering every voice
* Encouraging boldness
* Celebrating progress


 Accelerate the impact of our people by
   

  

* Amazing the client
* Prioritizing what matters
* Acting as one

title: Data Scientist - Energy Optimization
description:
10% of Europe's CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating, and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
   

  

 With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we're making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.
   

  

 Founded in 2022, and with 1000+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, an HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany, and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
   

  

**About The Role**
 In the Energy Optimization team, we build optimization and ML models that actively control when the heat pump runs, when the battery charges or discharges, and when to export energy to the grid. We are now building new flexibility capabilities by coordinating fleets of homes to support the grid when it matters most.
   

  

 We’re now looking for a Data Scientist to join the team. You’ll lead projects end-to-end: shape the problem, build forecasting and optimization models, run experiments, and work closely with Product, Engineering and Design to ship customer-facing AI solutions.
   

  

 We value clear communication, thoughtful collaboration, and a constructive approach to problem solving. If you enjoy making measurable progress, turning messy real-world signals into decisions people can trust, and moving projects forward even when the path is not fully defined, we would love to hear from you.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Translate real-world control problems into optimization models that minimizes customers’ energy bills.
* Develop and improve forecasting and decision-support models enabling optimal control schedules for customers’ home energy devices.
* Design and run experiments on real customer systems, evaluate impact, and turn insights into product decisions.
* Work closely with the team to bring models into production.
* Work across the full data stack, including data pipelines, feature engineering, deployment and monitoring.
* Lead initiatives from discovery to production impact. You set direction, align with stakeholders across product and engineering to deliver outcomes even when the path is not fully defined.
* Communicate clearly to technical and non-technical audiences, explaining trade-offs, uncertainty, and what is good enough to ship.


**What You Bring To The Team**
* Several years of experience as a Data Scientist, Optimization Engineer, Applied Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer or similar.
* Strong mathematical and statistical modelling skills.
* Experience with time-series forecasting and machine learning, ideally applied to operational systems.
* Experience working in Python and with modern ML/optimization tooling and workflows.
* Solid Software Engineering fundamentals for deploying and maintaining ML systems in production.
* A pragmatic, product-oriented mindset: you optimize for real-world impact, not model complexity.
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).


**Nice to have**
* Experience with constrained optimization and applying it to real-world systems.
* Experience with energy systems or electricity market knowledge, especially flexibility and demand response.
* Experience with control system or physical systems modelling.


**What is in it for you?**
* Gain hands-on experience in a fast-growing scale-up, with a supportive and inclusive culture, and career advancement opportunities.
* Collaborate with diverse teams across international markets and launch products globally.
* Develop and grow personally and professionally in an entrepreneurial-minded environment.
* Thrive in a dynamic, solution-oriented culture, tackling challenges and driving meaningful solutions that make a positive impact on the world.
* Influence the company's success, with a visible impact on product development and market growth.


 At Aira, We’re not just champions of next-generation energy, we’re champions in Diversity and Inclusion too. We believe that a diverse workforce sparks innovation and creativity and enables us to better serve our customers and communities. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. We will never accept any form of discrimination and believe that together, we can drive a new era for our people and planet.

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist
description:
**Job Description**
 Coherent Corp. is the global leader in photonics, empowering innovators to shape the future with breakthrough technologies, from advanced materials to complete laser and optical systems.
   

  

 Our solutions impact industrial, communications, electronics, and instrumentation markets, helping customers overcome complex challenges and unlock new possibilities. Headquartered in Saxonburg, Pennsylvania, we operate worldwide, offering an international, collaborative environment where research, manufacturing, and customer-focused teams drive innovation and deliver real-world results.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Järfälla
   

  

**Hybrid:** 
 3 days on-site per week
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Talent Acquisition Speciliast, EMEA is responsible for leading end-to-end talent acquisition strategy and delivery across the EMEA region, while playing a key role in the design, evolution, and maturity of the global Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence (CoE).
   

  

 This role partners with regional and global stakeholders to build scalable, data-driven, and inclusive hiring practices that support business growth, workforce planning, and long-term organisational capability.
   

  

 The successful candidate will help connect regional hiring needs into a more consistent, globally aligned talent model—balancing local market nuance with global standards.
   

  

 Please note we are looking for a Swedish speaking TA specialist.
   

  

**Roles & Responsibilities**
**Talent Acquisition**
* Oversee and drive all aspects of talent acquisition delivery across the EMEA region, supporting both professional and operational hiring needs.
* Partner with Business Leaders and HR to understand workforce plans, growth priorities, and critical hiring needs.
* Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders on hiring strategy, market conditions, and talent risks.
* Ensure high-quality candidate experience and strong hiring manager engagement across the region.


**Centre of Excellence (CoE) Design & Growth**
* Contribute to the design, build, and ongoing evolution of the global Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence
* Help define and embed global TA frameworks, processes, and governance.
* Champion a “one global TA team” mindset—driving collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and alignment across regions


**Process, Data & Capability Building**
* Drive continuous improvement of recruitment processes, technology, and ways of working
* Establish and track meaningful TA metrics to inform decision-making.
* Support the implementation and optimisation of ATS and recruitment technology platforms
* Build capability across TA teams through coaching, mentoring, and clear operating rhythms


**Employer Brand, DEI & Market Insight**
* Partner with Employer Branding and Communications teams to strengthen the organisation’s talent brand across EMEA markets
* Embed diversity, equity, and inclusion principles into hiring strategies and recruitment practices
* Monitor external talent market trends and competitor activity to proactively adapt hiring strategies


**About You**
* Bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience
* Proven experience leading talent acquisition at a regional or global level, ideally across Sweden
* Experience working within or contributing to a Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence model.
* Strong stakeholder management skills within complex, matrixed organisations.
* Data-driven mindset with experience using insights and metrics to inform hiring decisions
* Knowledge of diverse EMEA labor markets and hiring practices


**CULTURE COMMITMENT**
 Ensure adherence to company’s values (ICARE) in all aspects of your position at Coherent:
   

  

**I** 
 ntegrity – Create an Environment of Trust
   

  

**C** 
 ollaboration – Innovate Through the Sharing of Ideas
   

  

**A** 
 ccountability – Own the Process and the Outcome
   

  

**R** 
 espect – Recognize the Value in Everyone
   

  

**E** 
 nthusiasm – Find a Sense of Purpose in Work
   

  

 Current employees who wish to apply should submit a cover letter detailing their interest in the position and how their skills and experience align with the requirements of the role.
   

  

**About Us**
 Coherent is a global leader in lasers, engineered materials and networking components. We are a vertically integrated manufacturing company that develops innovative products for diversified applications in the industrial, optical communications, military, life sciences, semiconductor equipment, and consumer markets. Coherent provides a comprehensive career development platform within an environment that challenges employees to perform at their best, while rewarding excellence and hard-work through a competitive compensation program. It's an exciting opportunity to work for a company that offers stability, longevity and growth. Come Join Us!
   

  

**Note to recruiters and employment agencies:** 
 We will not pay for unsolicited resumes from recruiters and employment agencies unless we have a signed agreement and have required assistance, in writing, for a specific opening.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer (Steel Structures)
description:
**The Role**
 The Mechanical team at CorPower Ocean is looking for a Mechanical Design Engineer experienced in the design of large steel structures and industrial equipment to take a key role in the development and optimisation of our innovative wave energy converters.
   

  

 The role involves designing large, welded steel structures and mechanical systems for offshore environments, driving development from concept through testing and release for production.
   

  

 You will work closely with the mechanical design team but will also interact extensively with the wider organisation, including supply chain, manufacturing, testing and field installation teams, requiring strong collaboration skills.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design of large mechanical structures and welded steel assemblies (from concept to release-for-production)
* Present to cross-disciplinary stakeholders during design reviews
* Perform structural analysis (hand calculations and FEA)
* Produce technical documentation for internal use and for third-party verification and certification
* Perform failure mode, effects and criticality analysis for modules and systems
* Manage your systems and components to meet performance, schedule and budget targets
* Investigate improvements to performance, reliability and manufacturability
* Interface with requirement, supply chain, manufacturing and field-testing teams
* Support assembly, testing and pilot installations in the field
* Support and mentor junior engineers when needed


**Skills And Experience**
 You have 5+ years of experience designing large mechanical systems or welded steel structures, ideally in industries such as offshore, marine, renewable energy, heavy machinery, or industrial equipment.
   

  

 You have experience designing fabricated steel structures or heavy mechanical assemblies exposed to dynamic loads, fatigue, or demanding operating environments.
   

  

 You have experience with structural analysis of large fabricated or welded steel systems, exposed to dynamic loads, fatigue, or demanding operating environments.
   

  

 Experience contributing or leading end-to-end product development cycles from design, build, test to production in multiple projects.
   

  

 You have a broad interest in mechanical systems. Experience with mooring systems, gearboxes, hydraulics, structural optimisation, pressure vessels, fabrication methods, sensors or power generation equipment is considered an advantage.
   

  

 Experience with Creo CAD, Windchill PLM and ANSYS is beneficial; equivalent tools are also relevant.
   

  

 You may have a BSc or MSc degree in mechanical engineering or a related discipline, but practical experience and engineering mindset are most important.
   

  

 You are proficient in English, both spoken and written.
   

  

**As a Person, You**
* are striving for excellence, put quality into everything you do
* are self-motivated and constantly seek the most efficient ways to work
* search for simple and sustainable solutions to difficult problems
* love to learn new things, and take pride in constantly developing your skills
* are persistent, knowing it takes stamina to reach pioneering goals
* see your tasks through to completion
* are comfortable in presenting your solutions internally and externally
* are data driven and strive to always back your points with solid analysis


 CorPower Ocean has a diverse team with many nationalities and backgrounds, all working towards the same goal: To Power the Planet with clean energy from ocean waves.

title: Learning & Development Specialist
description:
**Bli en del av Coop – Tillsammans gör vi mat-Sverige hållbart!** 



  


 Vill du arbeta med lärande som utvecklar våra medarbetare och stärker Coops affär? Som Learning & Development Specialist på Coop skapar och driver du lärandeinitiativ som stärker förmågor och beteenden – alltid med målet att skapa kundvärde, bidra till lönsamhet och stödja Coops hållbara utveckling.
 


  


 Coop befinner sig på en viktig resa där kultur, rätt kompetens och lärande är avgörande för att driva verksamhetens förflyttning. Learning & Development har en viktig roll i att säkerställa att rätt förmågor finns på plats för att möta både nuvarande och framtida behov.
 


  


***Om tjänsten***



  


 Rollen som Learning & Development Specialist är både operativ och taktisk – från behovsanalys och design till genomförande, uppföljning och effektmätning.
 


  


 Med utgångspunkt i kundens och verksamhetens behov bidrar du till att utveckla lärande, arbetssätt och initiativ som stärker hela verksamheten. Du samarbetar brett inom organisationen, med butik, logistik, kategori och inköp, sälj, marknad samt centrala funktioner inom ekonomi och IT. På så sätt bidrar du till att lärande och lösningar fungerar i hela Coop och stärker både affären och kundupplevelsen.
 


  


 Du stöttar verksamheten i att omsätta verksamhetsbehov till konkreta lärandelösningar som fungerar i vardagen och bidrar till önskade förflyttningar.
 


  


**Det här kommer du att göra:** 



  

* Skapa och driva verksamhetsnära lärandeinitiativ för butik, föreningar, supportfunktioner och logistik med kundvärde i fokus
* Leda utvecklingsprojekt och workshops från behovsanalys till leverans, uppföljning och effektmätning
* Utveckla och producera lärresor och utbildningar i digitala, fysiska och blended format
* Arbeta i LMS och författarverktyg för att skapa användarnära och relevanta lärupplevelser
* Designa lärande som driver önskad beteende- och verksamhetsförflyttning
* Säkerställa tydlig kommunikation, förankring samt förvaltning och kvalitetssäkring av utbildningar
* Följa upp hur lärandet bidrar till kundvärde, beteendeförändring och affärsresultat


  


***Vem är du?***



  


 Vi söker dig som har flera års erfarenhet av kvalificerat arbete inom Learning & Development och som trivs med att arbeta nära verksamheten. Du tar ansvar från behov till effekt och drivs av att skapa lärande som gör skillnad i praktiken. Du har en god affärsförståelse och ser lärande som ett verktyg för att skapa värde för kund och verksamhet. Du är trygg i att samarbeta tvärfunktionellt och kan navigera i en komplex organisation. Du motiveras av att komma med nya idéer och att bidra till funktionens utveckling.
 


  


 Vidare så ser vi gärna att du har:
 


  

* Flera års erfarenhet inom L&D, kompetensutveckling eller motsvarande
* Erfarenhet av att utveckla utbildningar i olika format och arbeta med digitala lärmiljöer, LMS och författarverktyg
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leda beteendeförändring
* Erfarenhet av projektledning
* Förmåga att mäta, analysera och följa upp effekter av lärinsatser
* Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet från retail eller dagligvaruhandel.


  


**Ditt team och ditt ledarskap** 



  


 Du blir en del av ett nyfiket team med högt driv, där vi lär tillsammans, testar nytt och skapar värde för både människor och affär. Du rapporterar till mig, Monika Konjicki. Mitt ledarskap präglas av tydlig riktning, ett starkt fokus på kund och affär samt ett inkluderande och engagerande förhållningssätt.
 


  


***Vårt erbjudande till dig:***



  


 Hos oss får du möjlighet att arbeta i en verksamhet med tydligt samhällsengagemang och starkt hållbarhetsfokus. Du blir en del av ett engagerat HR-team och får arbeta i en roll med stort ansvar och bred kontaktyta inom organisationen. Vi erbjuder konkurrenskraftiga förmåner och en god balans mellan arbete och fritid.
 


  


**Så ansöker du:** 



  


 Låter detta som din nästa utmaning? Skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt då vi arbetar löpande med urval och intervjuer.
 


  


 Har du frågor om tjänsten är du varmt välkommen att kontakta rekryterande ledare Monika Konjicki på e-postadress monika.konjicki@coop.se.
 


  


 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!

title: Backend Engineer (Python/Go) - Consumer Platform team
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

**Are you excited about building the systems that keep Wolt running for millions of customers every day?** 
 We’re looking for a pragmatic
 **Backend Developer** 
 with strong skills in
 **Python or Go** 
 to join our
 **Consumer Platform** 
**team** 
 –the crew behind the core consumer infrastructure and reliability efforts that make Wolt fast, stable, and scalable.
   

  

 The
 **Consumer Platform team** 
 plays a central role within Wolt’s engineering organization. We design and maintain the backend components that power Wolt’s consumer-facing experience–from critical purchase flows to the underlying systems that ensure reliability at scale. The team combines hands-on software development with elements of DevOps and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), continuously improving how our systems perform and how our teams deliver.
   

  

 Known across Wolt for driving cross-team technical initiatives, the team works on challenges that impact the entire platform. Recent projects include scaling our purchase core to support split payments, developing an API gateway for Wolt’s most critical flows, and partnering with other teams to raise reliability standards company-wide.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Our team is primarily based in
 **Finland.**
**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Build and maintain high-throughput critical backend services in Python and Go
* Work across the complete development lifecycle—from architecture and design to deployment and monitoring
* Drive cross-team reliability initiatives and improve the scalability of Wolt’s platform
* Design and implement core services, shared libraries, internal tooling, and platform-level improvements that empower other teams and support our global consumer economy
* Define reliability best practices that make Wolt’s systems more robust


**Our Tech Stack:** 
 Python, Go, MongoDB, Redis, Datadog, CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes, AWS.
   

  

**Our humble expectations**
 We’re looking for someone with:
   

  

* Solid experience in backend development using Python, Go, or similar languages.
* A passion for core engineering problems like system design, optimization, and scalability.
* Experience working on high-traffic, production systems.
* (Bonus) Familiarity with reliability engineering or on-call practices.
* Strong ownership mindset and curiosity — someone who likes to understand how things work under the hood.
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate across teams
* A pragmatic, product-oriented mindset focused on solving real user problems


 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Data Scientist - Platform Developer Experience
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the frameworks, capabilities and tools needed to welcome a billion customers. Join us and help to amplify productivity, quality and innovation across Spotify.
   

  

 We are an insights team supporting across the domains of Developer Experience, Client Platform and Core Infrastructure. As a member of the team, you’ll work closely with a cross functional team of data scientists, data engineers, user researchers, and product managers who are all passionate about crafting scalable products for our developers and customers. Your focus will be in the Developer Experience domain which strives to bring a phenomenal experience for developers at Spotify, and through this deliver intuitive, stable and reliable products for people to enjoy.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Work closely with cross-functional teams of skilled engineers, data scientists, user researchers, designers, and product managers who are all passionate about providing an outstanding product experience
* Define metrics, build dashboards and compose reports, empowering data-informed improvements to developer experience.
* Perform exploratory analysis to understand how engineers interact with our internal developer platform, how they derive value from deployment and efficiency tooling, and where we can evolve the developer experience to unlock greater efficiency and innovation.
* Communicate insights and recommendations to key partners, helping activate data best practices


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have a 4+ years work experience with an emphasis on programmatic data analysis, reporting, and visualization/ dashboarding with Tableau or similar BI solution
* You are able to communicate technical information clearly and concisely, tailoring to different audiences.
* You are curious and not afraid of exploring new domains; you enjoy partnering with others to define and develop new opportunities
* You have shown experience applying coding skills such as Python, SQL, and/or R to analyze complex data
* You have experience or a strong interest in internal developer platforms and tooling, with exposure to domains such as continuous integration, deployment pipelines, documentation systems, developer education, and generative AI productivity tools.


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm.
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Finansinspektionen söker studenter som vill extrajobba med teknisk support
description:
Är du student på högskola eller universitet inom teknik och är intresserad av ett extrajobb på en spännande myndighet? Nu söker Finansinspektionen (FI) studenter till vår it-support!
   

  

**Om jobbet**
 Som studentmedarbetare på avdelningen Applikationsdrift och it-support hjälper du till och stöttar upp i supporten. Arbetsuppgifter inkluderar bland annat installation av nya skärmar, uppdatera it-guider på myndighetens intranät, iordningsställa arbetsplatser och konferensrum, samt att bistå it-supporten med andra enklare uppgifter.
   

  

**Din utbildning och erfarenhet**
 Vi söker dig som
   

  

* går på universitet/högskola med teknisk inriktning och avklarat minst 60 hp
* kan arbeta cirka två dagar i veckan vid sidan av dina studier
* är teknikintresserad och van att hantera datorer och mobiltelefoner
* har tidigare arbetslivserfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter.


 Det är meriterande om du
   

  

* har arbetat eller praktiserat på myndighet/ bolags it-verksamhet.


**Om dig**
 I det här jobbet behöver du kunna
   

  

* bidra till ett gott samarbete
* agera och ta beslut när det behövs
* ta egna initiativ
* vara hjälpsam, flexibel och bemöta andra på ett vänligt och respektfullt sätt.


**Om FI**
 FI bidrar till ett stabilt finansiellt system, en hållbar utveckling och ett högt skydd för konsumenter. Vi erbjuder dig att arbeta med samhällsviktiga uppgifter, där du är med och påverkar den finansiella marknaden.
   

  

 På FI arbetar cirka 600 personer och våra värderingar präglas av att vi är engagerade, beslutsamma, målinriktade och hjälpsamma. Vår arbetsplats ligger centralt i Stockholm. Det finns även möjlighet att delvis arbeta på distans.
   

  

 Verksamhetsområde It leder det strategiska digitaliseringsarbetet, driver innovation och ansvarar för internationell koordinering inom it. Vårt uppdrag är brett och vårt ansvarsområde sträcker sig från löpande drift, support och säkerhet till utveckling och förvaltning av våra system, projektledning, arkitektur, upphandlingar samt leverantörsstyrning. Här jobbar cirka 100 personer, fördelade på sex avdelningar och en stab.
   

  

**Bra att veta**
 Vi baserar vår rekrytering på kompetens. Det innebär att vi noggrant definierar vilka kvalifikationer som efterfrågas och sedan utgår från dem genom hela rekryteringsprocessen.
   

  

 Vi arbetar aktivt för mångfald och likabehandling och välkomnar därför medarbetare med olika erfarenheter, bakgrunder och kompetenser.
   

  

 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 På FI arbetar vi aktivt för att vara en attraktiv arbetsgivare där du som medarbetare trivs och utvecklas. Läs mer om hur det är att jobba på FI och vad vi erbjuder: https://www.fi.se/sv/om-fi/lediga-jobb/vi-erbjuder/
   

  

**Sök jobbet**
 Välkommen med din ansökan som ska innehålla cv och relevanta intyg. Vi använder urvalsfrågor som en del i rekryteringsprocessen.
   

  

 Vi avböjer all hjälp med rekrytering och annonsering.
   

  

**Anställningsform:**
 Anställningen är en tidsbegränsad anställning vid behov och med tillträde efter överenskommelse. Anställning sker terminsvis. Under sommarperioden juni-augusti finns eventuell möjlighet till anställning.
   

  

**Omfattning:**
 Deltid
   

  

**Kontaktperson:**
 Joakim Sandberg, avdelningschef, 08-408 980 93
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag:**
 2026-03-20

title: Quality Engineer – Shipbuilding
description:
**Professional Galaxy** 
 is an IT and technology consulting company that provides highly specialized expertise within IT, software development, SAP, purchasing, electronics and mechanical design. We collaborate with experienced senior experts and deliver strategic value-creating expertise to some of Sweden's most complex and analytically demanding projects. Our focus is always on high quality, professionalism and clear, measurable results.
   

  

 We are now seeking a
 **Quality Engineer – Shipbuilding** 
 for one of our clients.
   

  

**Assignment Description﻿**
 We are looking for a quality engineer with experience from the marine or shipbuilding industry who can be responsible for and carry out quality inspections in connection with new construction, conversion and maintenance of ships in shipyards.
   

  

 The role includes independently planning, coordinating and carrying out inspections of, among other things, welding work, pipe installations, electrical installations and other common work in ship production. You ensure that the work is carried out in accordance with current drawings, technical specifications, classification requirements, international standards and the customer's quality requirements.
   

  

**Relevant knowledge/experience:**
 Carry out visual and technical inspection of welds (e.g. according to WPS/WPQR and relevant ISO/EN standards)
   

  

 Inspection of piping systems including pressure testing and verification against P&ID and isometrics
   

  

 Review and control of electrical installations according to applicable regulations and marine standards
   

  

 Follow-up of deviations, documentation and reporting to project management and customer
   

  

 Participation in FAT, SAT and other verification activities
   

  

 Ensuring that the work meets the requirements of classification societies and authorities
   

  

 Coordination with production, project management and external inspectors
   

  

 We would like you to have good knowledge of quality processes, documentation and traceability, as well as experience of working in international project environments. Certifications in welding inspection (e.g. IWI/IWT/IWE) or experience with NDT are meritorious.
   

  

 The role requires high accuracy, technical understanding and the ability to work independently in a production-intensive environment.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm
   

  

**Start date:** 
 ASAP
   

  

**End date:** 
 Open
   

  

**Application Deadline:** 
 2026-03-30
   

  

 Are you the right person for the assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate? Do not hesitate to contact us.
   

  

 Please apply directly through our system with:
   

  

* Your updated CV in english
* Availability to start the assignment


 In the motivation, describe why you are suitable for this assignment - refer to previous consulting assignments, employmxent, education and personal qualities.
   

  

**Please note:** 
 We do not accept any applications through mail. All applications have to be sent through the portal to be valid.
   

  

**Offer continuously:** 
 Please note that for this role we offer continuously. That means that we sometimes remove the assignments before the deadline. If you are interested, we recommend that you apply immediately.

title: Gameplay Engineer – LLMs, Creator Tools & Narrative Systems
description:
As a Gameplay Engineer on Embark's Machine Learning team, you will help shape the future of storytelling tools and systems for video games.
   

  

 We believe modern AI can unlock entirely new creative workflows for narrative design, quest building, lore creation, and player-facing reactivity. Our goal is to investigate how we can design new tools and systems that enable creators to be more ambitious, collaborate more effectively, and explore ideas with less friction rather than simply automating existing workflows.
   

  

 In this role, you will build gameplay-facing systems, tools, and integrations that make AI useful in real production environments—practical, controllable, debuggable, and aligned with the creative vision. You'll work closely with writers, researchers, designers, and other engineers to explore different levels of AI agency to intentionally design where AI agency makes sense and where it doesn't.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who is excited about creative tooling, loves building things that people actually use, and is genuinely enthusiastic about what new AI techniques can enable in games.
   

  

**Example Of Responsibilities**
* Build gameplay systems and tools that enable new narrative workflows and faster iteration for creators
* Collaborate with designers and narrative teams to understand creative needs and turn them into intuitive tooling
* Develop engine integrations and "hooks" that expose the right information for analysis, validation, and experimentation
* Create debugging and diagnostics workflows that make complex systems understandable and easy to iterate on
* Work with writers and designers to explore and prototype AI-native gameplay mechanics that preserve authorial intent while enabling emergence and reactivity
* Prototype experimental features quickly, then help mature the best ideas into robust production-ready solutions
* Help design and evaluate different degrees of AI autonomy, balancing creative ambition, reliability, and control
* Help ensure AI-assisted workflows remain controllable, transparent, and human-driven
* Work across disciplines to integrate tools into real game development pipelines, not isolated demos


**We would love if you have**
* Strong gameplay engineering experience and excellent C++ skills
* Experience working in Unreal Engine
* A passion for tooling, iteration speed, and developer experience
* A creator mindset: you enjoy building systems that make other people more effective
* Comfort collaborating with designers and translating creative goals into technical solutions
* Professional English communication skills


**Additionally, It's a Bonus If You Have**
* Experience building editor tools or content workflows in Unreal Engine
* Familiarity with language models, AI tooling, evaluation, or "human-in-the-loop" creative systems
* Experience with narrative systems, quest pipelines, or other creator-driven game features
* Python experience, or interest in working across engine + services/tooling


 At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
   

  

 We welcome game makers of all sex, class, colour, age, gender identity, education, religion, opinion, culture, nation of origin, language, sexual orientation, shape, size, and ability.
   

  

 Did we leave anyone out? Well, we welcome you, too! We think that the gaming industry is made better when everyone has a seat at the table.
   

  

 Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

title: Lead Data Engineer
Product Designer, FW
description:
**Job Description**
*The future of gaming in Finland is changing – and we’re recruiting. In 2027, the Finnish license market will open, bringing a new era for iGaming. At Veikkaus, we’re preparing for this transformation - and we’re looking for passionate professionals to join us on this exciting journey.*
*We’re strengthening our Betting and iCasino teams with true iGaming experts: people with international experience, a hunger for success, and a persistent drive to win. If you’re ready to help shape the future of responsible, competitive money gaming, now is the time to join us.*
**Product Designer**
 We’re looking for a
 **Product Designer – Framework (FW)** 
 to create seamless user journeys and high-quality interfaces across our digital product experience, support user testing and develop our design system.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with product designers, product owners, and developers to deliver intuitive solutions that improve the look, feel and usability of our digital experiences while creating interactions that feel seamless, intuitive, and well-structured.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Design user flows, wireframes, and high-fidelity UI for core platform experiences and shared user journeys, such as account, onboarding, payments, navigation, and common UX patterns.
* Support user testing activities and incorporate feedback into designs.
* Contribute to and maintain the design system for consistency and scalability.
* Deliver high-quality user flows, concepts, prototypes, and design documentation.


**Qualifications And Experience**
* 3+ years of experience in product or UX design.
* Proficiency in Figma and design tools for creating production ready designs, user flows, wireframes, and prototypes.
* Understanding of design systems and multi-platform UX principles.
* Ability to collaborate and integrate feedback from stakeholders and users.
* Experience in the iGaming industry and especially Sports betting is highly valued.
* Proven international track record in B2C products.
* Experience in cross-platform products, including native iOS (Swift), native Android (Kotlin), mobile web, and desktop web is required.
* Bachelor’s degree in Design, HCI, or related field, or equivalent experience.


**We Offer**
* A workplace where the game evolves – and so do you. You’ll face meaningful challenges and gain new skills while contributing to shared success – in Finland and beyond. We value curiosity and create the space for it to thrive.
* Inspiring and supportive teammates you can rely on. The best ideas and world-class entertainment are built together – and Veikkaus thrives because of its people.
* Balanced and inspiring work life. At Veikkaus, we believe a good job adds something to life – but isn’t all there is to it.
* Excellent employee benefits. You can read more about us and our benefits from here.


 It is important to us that everyone can come to work as their authentic selves. We value diverse backgrounds, skills, and perspectives, and believe that success comes from working in diverse teams. We encourage individuals from various backgrounds to apply for our jobs.
   

  

 The position is located at Veikkaus’ headquarters in Pohjois-Haaga, Helsinki. We work flexibly in a hybrid model. The position is permanent and full-time.
   

  

**Interested?** 
 Awesome! Please send your application with your portfolio, CV and salary request no later than
 **29.03.2026.** 
 We are excited to hear from you!
   

  

 For any inquiries about the position don't hesitate to contact Sanna Shibasaki-Rosenberg, Head of Design at sanna.shibasaki-rosenberg@veikkaus.fi
   

  

*A drug test under Section 7 of the Act on the Protection of Privacy in Working Life (759/2004) will be conducted on the selected candidate.*
**About Us**
 Veikkaus on jokaisen suomalaisten oma peliyhtiö, joka kehittää vastuullista ja viihdyttävää rahapelaamista. Tarjoamme merkityksellisiä tehtäviä, kasvun mahdollisuuksia ja työyhteisön, jossa välittäminen, yhdessä tekeminen ja rohkeus näkyvät arjessa. Haluamme, että jokainen voi kehittyä, onnistua ja olla oma itsensä – samalla kun rakennamme menestystä yhdessä.
   

  

 Paremman pelin puolesta – ja työelämän.

title: Internship | Transaction Diligence | Fall 2026 | Stockholm & Gothenburg
description:
**How will we work better together to build a better working world?**
 EY-Parthenon’s Transaction Diligence team is looking for students to join our teams in Stockholm and Gothenburg for a full-time internship for a period of 8-12 weeks with flexible dates.
   

  

 EY-Parthenon advises clients on all aspects of M&A transactions. Our transaction professionals work in an international environment and cover a wide spectrum of industries. Teaming is crucial to our success. During your internship, you can expect full support from great colleagues, a buddy to help you settle in and a supervisor who provides you with continuous feedback and follows your development.
   

  

**The opportunity: your next adventure awaits**
**Responsibilities**
 Our clients are the leading Nordic and international private equity houses and corporates. We often work closely together with the major investment banks, strategy houses and law firms as part of a wider team of advisors to create value for our clients. Our responsibilities include:
   

  

* Establish and evaluate businesses’ underlying profitability
* Identify and understand key business drivers
* Establish and evaluate normalized net working capital requirements
* Identify possible risks related to the transaction and advise our client on how to mitigate them
* Calculate adjustments to the purchase price and evaluate closing mechanisms


**What We Look For**
* Analytical mindset and interest in financial analysis
* Goal-oriented and striving to deliver exceptional results
* Ability to prioritize tasks and work under tight deadlines
* Team player with excellent social skills to work in a multicultural and fast-paced environment
* Good command of Microsoft Office tools


**Qualifications**
* Future degree within Finance, Accounting or Economics with good academic results


- 

* Future degree within (Industrial) Management, Engineering or Mathematics can also be considered, provided they have extracurriculars or prior work experience in related fields (finance and accounting)
* Fluent in English and Swedish or another Nordic language


**What’s In It For You**
* Accelerate your technical capabilities and transformative leadership skills with future-focused courses and development programs.
* Broaden your horizons by working on highly integrated teams across the globe and collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds — both professionally and culturally.
* Bring out the best in yourself with continuous investment in your personal well-being and career development.
* Develop your own personal purpose and help us create a positive ripple effect on our teams, our business, clients and society.


**Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today.**
 Please submit your application including your CV, cover letter, and university transcripts. Selection and interviews will be conducted on an ongoing basis.
   

  

 If you have questions about the role, please contact Nathalie.jonoska@parthenon.ey.com. For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Amanda.hederstedt@se.ey.com.
   

  

 Please note that a background check will be conducted as part of the recruitment process.
   

  

 At EY, we value diversity and encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds. If you have any questions, need support, or require adjustments to the recruitment process due to a disability, don’t hesitate to contact us at recruitmentsweden@se.ey.com. We are here to support you.

title: Account Executive: International Sales
description:
**About Magma** 




 Magma is one of the world’s fastest-growing and most celebrated edtech companies, transforming math education through the power of AI. Our platform turns handwritten solutions into digital insights, empowering teachers to focus on creativity and deeper learning, while giving them real-time visibility into student progress.
 



 The result? More inclusive, engaging, and effective math classrooms for all learners.
 



 But we’re not just building better tools. We’re tackling one of the biggest challenges in global education: math underperformance. With 1 in 5 students globally struggling in math, a subject that forms the backbone of so many career paths, we believe the opportunity to improve outcomes at scale is massive.
 



 We’re already the go-to solution in thousands of schools, and we’re just getting started. As we expand rapidly across the U.S. and Europe, we’re building a team as ambitious as our mission: to help millions of students unlock their full potential in math.
 



  





**The Role** 




 Magma is a sales driven organisation and the sales team enables us to reach hundreds of thousands of teachers and pupils on a daily basis. As an Account Executive you will be responsible for sourcing new leads and closing deals with schools and organisations. You will have a large amount of autonomy in how to drive your pipe forward, but also have continuous support from colleagues.
 



 You will be attending trade shows and client networking events to find meaningful opportunities. To close deals it is necessary to build relationships with teachers and pedagogical staff as well as the most senior leadership. We have a fun and energetic culture and you will support in building and conveying this culture to customers.
 



 You will have a large impact on how our International sales team functions and grows. This is a fantastic opportunity to join early in a journey where there is already a world leading product, strong traction and supportive team in place.
 



  





**Who You Are And What You Know** 




 To succeed in the role you need to:
 


* Be very ambitious and like chasing challenging targets
* Like doing business and interacting with decision makers
* Be a confident and trustworthy person towards senior decision makers as well as teachers and pedagogical staff
* Be sociable and enjoy trade shows and networking events



**Requirements** 



* Experience as Account Executive in B2G or B2B preferably towards education or in SaaS
* A track record of closing deals and interacting with senior leadership
* Bachelor's Degree in any field is a strong plus



  





**What We Offer** 



* Play a key role in an exciting journey. We are a young company with ambitious targets and meaningful goals
* Fantastic colleagues in a fun environment with great opportunities to learn
* Competitive remuneration & benefit package
* Annual company trip
* An opportunity to work on a meaningful mission and have an exceptional impact on education, society and how children learn math across the world



**Start** 
 : As soon as possible
 



**Location** 
 : Stockholm
 



  





**Our Recruitment Process** 




 1st interview with Hiring Manager and Head of People
 



 2nd interview and case with VP Revenue and Account Executive
 



 3rd interview with Founder and Head of Curriculum

title: iOS Engineer
description:
At Svea Solar, we have a clear vision – to fix the planet. As Sweden’s and one of Europe’s leading players in energy technology, we are driving the energy transition with solar power and smart energy solutions. ⚡
   

  

 We’re now expanding our app team with an
 **iOS Engineer** 
 . Are you ready to join the Power Shift?
   

  

**About The Role & The Team**
 As our iOS Engineer, you’ll help shape our new app and its extensions, including the Apple Watch app. The app already supports tens of thousands (soon hundreds of thousands 😉) customers in optimizing their energy use. Our iOS app is built with SwiftUI and Swift concurrency, connected to a newly developed backend, using Apple’s latest frameworks. The minimum iOS version is 16 (but is being bumped to 18 this fall).
   

  

 You’ll work closely with our iOS Staff Engineer, Product Manager, and the rest of the app team to define and deliver on our roadmap, reporting to the Engineering Manager.
   

  

*We follow a collaboration-first policy, working three days a week from our Alvik office.*
**Your key responsibilities:**
* Take part in defining the architecture, platform, and best practices for the development of our apps on iOS and watchOS
* Write, review, and test code in a collaborative environment with other software engineers
* Work closely with other engineers, designers, and product owners to explore new solutions to problems
* Contribute to the development roadmap on how to best further us on our way to rid the planet of fossil fuels


 Are we a match?
   

  

**We’re Looking For You Who…**
* Values team success over personal success.
* Are fluent in English - our organizational language.
* Have extensive experience in Swift, with solid knowledge of Swift concurrency and SwiftUI.
* Write readable, idiomatic, and maintainable Swift, and are happy to follow established coding guidelines and workflows.
* Have released at least one app on the App Store, or you have a project in the works you can show us.


**Bonus points if you:**
* Stays up to date with advancements in Swift 6.


 🌍 Life as a Power Shifter - What do we offer you
   

  

**The opportunity to make an impact** 
 – your work directly contributes to fixing the planet and driving the energy transition.
   

  

**About**
**A value-driven workplace** 
 – read more about our values here.
   

  

**The chance to own your results** 
 – grow, take responsibility, and power up as both we and the energy industry evolve.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusivity** 
 – different perspectives drive innovation. Here, you can be yourself and contribute with your experiences, identities, and ideas.
   

  

**Employment benefits** 
 – occupational pension, 30 days of vacation, wellness allowance, parking, and more.
   

  

**Flexibility/ hybrid work** 
 – work up to two days a week from home.
   

  

 Want to see what life at Svea Solar really looks like? Follow us on Instagram: @LifeAtSveaSolar
   

  

 ⚡Who are we
   

  

 As Sweden’s - and one of Europe’s - leading providers of energy tech and solar, we help our customers optimize their energy use with smart, connected solutions. From solar panels to heat pumps, batteries, EV chargers, and software, we’re shaping the future of energy. We call it
 **The Power Shift** 
 . And the ones driving it?
 **Power Shifters** 
 .
   

  

  

  

**About Our Recruitment Process**
* With structured interviews and assessments through Alva Labs, we make sure our recruitment process is fair, inclusive, and unbiased - because diversity is key to innovation!
* Read more about our Recruitment process here.


 👉
 **Ready to become a Power Shifter?** 
 We review applications on a rolling basis, so don’t wait to send yours in!

title: Genesys Cloud Specialist
description:
B2B/Freelance - Market Rate - Hybrid in Stockholm
 



  





**About the Role** 




 We are seeking an experienced
 **Genesys Cloud Architect / Senior Developer** 
 to join on a contract basis. The successful candidate will play a key role in designing, developing, and optimizing customer engagement solutions within Genesys Cloud. This role requires strong technical expertise, the ability to work independently, and excellent communication skills to collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders.

* Design, architect, and implement solutions on the

 .
* Develop and customize call flows, IVR, routing strategies, and integrations with third-party systems.

 .
