 You will report to and work closely with our
 **Data & Analytics Lead** 
 . Together, you will form the core of Auctionet’s data function, combining strong analytical understanding with solid engineering foundations.
   

  

 You will also collaborate closely with our DevOps and product engineering teams, especially around platform decisions and performance.
   

  

 Auctionet has around 170 employees and is the parent company of a wider group with approximately 400 colleagues across multiple countries. Our main office is near Östermalmstorg in central Stockholm, with logistics centers in Malmö, Stockholm, Gothenburg, Westbury, and Cologne.
   

  

**Our Data Platform (Why This Role Is Different)** 
 For compliance, cost, and performance reasons, we run most of our analytics workloads in a
 **self-hosted Kubernetes cluster,** 
 built together with our DevOps team.
   

  

**Current stack includes:** 
 Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, Dagster, dbt, Python, and Metabase. You’ll also help shape our tooling going forward.
   

  

 This means:
   

  

* We are not constrained by traditional cloud pricing models
* We have access to large compute resources when needed
* We can tune performance deeply — from queries to storage


 At the same time, this setup is more hands-on and less standardized than managed cloud solutions. We’re looking for someone who finds this
 **interesting rather than scary** 
 - someone who wants to understand how things work under the hood and isn’t afraid of a bit of complexity.
   

  

**About You** 
 You’re either:
   

  

* an infra-curious data engineer who enjoys databases, performance, and platform topics and wants to work closer to analytics, or
* an analyst-curious engineer who cares deeply about data models, business logic, and usability, but also wants to understand the infrastructure that powers it all.


 To succeed in this role, we think you have:
   

  

* Previous experience as a Data Engineer (or similar role)
* Strong SQL and Python skills
* Hands-on experience with dbt
* Experience building and maintaining analytical data warehouses
* A good understanding of data modeling, testing, and data quality
* Interest in infrastructure, performance, and operating data systems


**Bonus and not mandatory:**
* Background as a database engineer, or deep knowledge of databases like PostgreSQL
* Understanding of query planning, indexing, and database internals
* Experience working with Kubernetes or self-hosted data platforms
* Experience with orchestration tools such as Dagster or Airflow
* Experience working closely with analysts or BI teams


**Our Recruitment Process** 
 We have a straightforward process without unpleasant surprises.
   

  

 We’ll start with a short phone interview to get a sense of whether we could be a good match for each other.
   

  

 Then we’ll have a few meetings (usually two or three) where we talk about you, the role, and Auctionet — and you’ll get the chance to meet the people you’ll work closely with. As part of the process, we’ll also ask you to do a small take-home exercise (2-3 hours). In the end, we hope it feels like the perfect fit for both of us.
   

  

**Apply By** 
 We will be reviewing applications continuously, but please submit your application asap
   

  

**Who We Are** 
 Auctionet was founded in 2011 by a team of people with unique experience of online auctions.
   

  

 In 2021, Auctionet acquired the auction house Stockholms Auktionsverk, and we are now by far the largest auction marketplace in the Nordics, and one of the larger auction marketplaces in the world. With this acquisition, Auctionet owns and operates both the oldest auction house in the world (1674) and the second oldest (1681), which means we have been working with sustainability for 350(!) years.
   

  

 Join us in our vision to make valued objects accessible for everyone, from everywhere!

title: Country Manager, Nordics
description:
We’re in an unbelievably exciting area of tech and are fundamentally reshaping the data storage industry. Here, you lead with innovative thinking, grow along with us, and join the smartest team in the industry.
   

  

 This type of work—work that changes the world—is what the tech industry was founded on. So, if you're ready to seize the endless opportunities and leave your mark, come join us.
   

  

**THE ROLE**
 As the Nordic Country Manager, you will be the strategic catalyst for Pure Storage’s "lightspeed" growth across the region. You will lead a high-performing multi-functional team, serving as a talent magnet and culture carrier to embed our values into every local operation. Reporting to the EMEA Leadership team, your mission is to transform our regional market presence through executive-level engagement and a sophisticated channel ecosystem.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Scale and Inspire: Architect a world-class sales organization by recruiting top-tier talent and fostering a high-performance culture that reflects Pure’s authentic values.
* Drive Strategic Growth: Deliver predictable business results, including annual bookings goals and new logo production, while maintaining a rigorous 4X pipeline to ensure long-term territory health.
* Lead the Ecosystem: Orchestrate the "extended" Pure team—including SEs, Channel Managers, and Inside Sales—to execute complex on-premise and cloud sales strategies.
* Cultivate Partnerships: Build and maintain high-impact relationships with channel partner principals and leadership teams to expand our market reach and influence.
* Operational Excellence: Lead through data-driven performance management, utilizing analytical skills and CRM tools to optimize sales activity, forecast accuracy, and POC management.


**What You Bring**
* Visionary Leadership: A proven ability to lead technology sales teams with a charismatic and persuasive style that inspires collective achievement.
* Strategic Sales Expertise: Mastery of the IT infrastructure landscape, with the ability to navigate highly competitive environments and engage effectively at the C-Suite level.
* Collaborative Influence: Exceptional interpersonal skills to align internal stakeholders across all levels of the organization and lead cooperative efforts across functional boundaries.
* Bilingual Communication: Mastery of English and business-fluent Dutch is required to effectively articulate Pure’s value proposition to a diverse regional audience.
* Operational Rigor: A process-oriented mindset with the ability to identify root causes, solve complex problems, and manage multiple strategic priorities simultaneously.
* Location: We are primarily an in-office environment and therefore, you will be expected to work from the Stockholm office in compliance with Pure’s policies, unless you are on PTO, or work travel, or other approved leave.


**What You Can Expect From Us**
* Innovation: We celebrate those who think critically, like a challenge, and aspire to be trailblazers.
* Growth: We give you the space and support to grow along with us and to contribute to something meaningful. We have been named Fortune's Best Workplaces in Technology™, Fortune's Best Workplaces in the Bay Area™, and certified as a Great Place to Work®!
* Team: We build each other up and set aside ego for the greater good.


 And because we understand the value of bringing your full and best self to work, we offer a variety of perks to manage a healthy balance, including flexible time off, wellness resources, and company-sponsored team events. Check out purebenefits.com for more information.
   

  

**Accommodations And Accessibility**
 Candidates with disabilities may request accommodations for all aspects of our hiring process. For more on this, contact us at TA-Ops@purestorage.com if you’re invited to an interview.
   

  

**Our Commitment To a Strong And Inclusive Team**
 We’re forging a future where everyone finds their rightful place and where every voice matters. Where uniqueness isn’t just accepted but embraced. That’s why we are committed to fostering the growth and development of every person, cultivating a sense of community through our Employee Resource Groups and advocating for inclusive leadership.
   

  

 Everpure is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or any other characteristic legally protected by the laws of the jurisdiction in which you are being considered for hire.
   

  

**Join us and bring your best.**
**Bring your bold.**
**Pure and simple.**

title: Supporttekniker Service Desk SE B2B
description:
**Supporttekniker Service Desk**
 Vill du arbeta nära nätet som är den digitala ryggraden i samhället?
   

  

 Som Supporttekniker i Service Desk hos GlobalConnect är du med och säkerställer stabil och tillförlitlig kommunikation för våra B2B-kunder – varje dag.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 I rollen som Supporttekniker i dagteamet arbetar du mitt i hjärtat av vår nätverksamhet. Du hanterar larm, felsöker incidenter och ser till att våra tjänster är tillgängliga när samhället behöver dem.
   

  

 Du arbetar tillsammans med kollegor i Service Desk och 24/7-teamet och ansvarar för att övervaka, felsöka och koordinera ärenden. Tempot kan vara högt och du hanterar flera parallella ärenden samtidigt – alltid med fokus på kvalitet och kund.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter**
* Felsöka nätverksärenden inom Fortinet, SD-WAN, routing och säkerhet
* Hantera kundincidenter vid larm eller avbrott
* Supportera kunder, kollegor och underleverantörer via ärendehanteringssystem
* Eskalera och fördela ärenden för korrekt prioritering och arbetsfördelning
* Dokumentera och ta fram rapporter (RFO/RCA) vid större incidenter
* Bidra till förbättring av rutiner och arbetssätt inom Service Desk


**För att lyckas i rollen behöver du**
* Relevant utbildning inom nätverk, gärna på högskolenivå
* CCNA-certifiering eller stark motivation att ta CCNA
* 1–2 års erfarenhet från Service Desk, Help Desk eller liknande teknisk supportroll
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


 Du är strukturerad, ansvarstagande och analytisk. Du har ett tydligt kundfokus och samarbetar gärna med andra och trivs när tempot är högt.
   

  

**Teamet och varför GlobalConnect**
 GlobalConnect bygger, äger och driver den digitala infrastrukturen som förbinder företag och samhällen i Norden. Vårt nät är samhällskritisk infrastruktur – när det fungerar märks det knappt, men när det ligger nere påverkas sjukhus, kommuner och företag direkt.
   

  

 Som Supporttekniker är du en del av den kedja som säkrar tillgänglighet, kvalitet och stabilitet. Rollen ger dig en stark teknisk grund och möjlighet att utvecklas vidare inom nätverk, säkerhet eller andra delar av verksamheten om du så önskar framöver.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande och ser fram emot att höra från dig!
   

  

**About Us**
 GlobalConnect is one of the leading connectivity providers in Northern Europe. We’re 2000 passionate and talented individuals who want to make a difference, turning our customers’ visions into reality through connectivity. Our goal is to create the best possible conditions for engaged employees, a place where you can develop and grow, and create unforgettable memories and enjoyable experiences.
   

  

 We have helped develop and digitize societies across the Nordics for more than two decades. Our purpose is what drives us to continue - turning visions into reality by empowering society with connectivity.

title: Senior Product Manager, ZellisONE
description:
We are hiring for
 **Zellis** 
 , a fast-moving technology company. Zellis and Benifex are sister companies, both operating under in the Zellis group.
 



  





 ZellisONE combines HR, Workforce Management, Pay, Talent and Benefits in one AI-enabled, configurable platform. Trusted to pay more than two million employees every month, we help organisations run payroll at scale, while unifying the full people experience.
 



 Our vision is to be the clear leader in pay, reward, analytics, and people experiences. We're passionate about creating an environment where people want to join, belong to, and be part of a progressive organisation.
 



  





**Why should you apply? 🌟** 



* Your chance to lead the end‑to‑end expansion of ZellisONE into the Swedish payroll & HR technology market
* Shape localisation strategy, product design, and go‑to‑market execution for an entirely new geography
* Become the organisation’s voice of the Swedish customer, influencing senior stakeholders and product strategy
* Work for a profitable, fast‑growing market leader in the HR Technology arena



  





**Please note we are unable to offer visa sponsorship and require to people to be based in Sweden for this position** 




  





**Role Overview 🚀** 




 As our
 **Senior Product Manager** 
 for Sweden, you will lead the localisation, specification, and commercial success of ZellisONE in the Swedish market. You’ll transform customer, market, and regulatory insight into product strategy, define Sweden‑specific capabilities, collaborate closely with engineering and design and be a key voice in the go‑to‑market process.
 



 This is a unique opportunity to build something from the ground up, ensuring our product is market‑fit, compliant, and loved by Swedish users.
 



  





**Responsibilities 🤘** 



* Serve as the internal Swedish market expert, bringing deep insight into HR, payroll, tax, and employment legislation
* Conduct research, interviews and discovery to shape requirements for Swedish‑specific product functionality
* Own the end‑to‑end specification of localised features, writing epics, features and user stories
* Partner with engineering and design to deliver high‑quality, compliant, fit‑for‑purpose solutions
* Drive prioritisation using customer value, commercial impact, and technical constraints
* Shape Sweden’s go‑to‑market strategy, including positioning, messaging and launch readiness
* Support Sales with demos, customer conversations, and RFP responses
* Track post‑launch adoption and commercial performance, refining the product direction
* Build strong stakeholder relationships across Product, Engineering, Compliance, Sales, Marketing and Leadership



  





**What are we looking for? 🔎** 



* Extensive experience in HR Technology, working to improve SaaS products for customers across the Nordic region
* A strong understanding of the Swedish HR, payroll, and employment landscape
* Experience launching products into new European markets
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complexity
* A proactive problem‑solver who thrives in ambiguity and leads cross‑functional teams effectively
* Fluency in English & Swedish



  





**Our interview process** 




 We understand the need to have a fast and efficient process, the below will all be completed in the shortest time possible.
 



 📞 Initial call with the Talent team
 



 ⏳ Interviews with the hiring manager and key stakeholders
 



 🤩 Final interview with the Zellis CEO
 



  





**We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.** 




  





**We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels welcome and respected. We believe that diversity and inclusion are essential to our success, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.**

title: Data Scientist - Energy Optimization
description:
10% of Europe's CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating, and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
   

  

 With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we're making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.
   

  

 Founded in 2022, and with 1000+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, an HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany, and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
   

  

**About The Role**
 In the Energy Optimization team, we build optimization and ML models that actively control when the heat pump runs, when the battery charges or discharges, and when to export energy to the grid. We are now building new flexibility capabilities by coordinating fleets of homes to support the grid when it matters most.
   

  

 We’re now looking for a Data Scientist to join the team. You’ll lead projects end-to-end: shape the problem, build forecasting and optimization models, run experiments, and work closely with Product, Engineering and Design to ship customer-facing AI solutions.
   

  

 We value clear communication, thoughtful collaboration, and a constructive approach to problem solving. If you enjoy making measurable progress, turning messy real-world signals into decisions people can trust, and moving projects forward even when the path is not fully defined, we would love to hear from you.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Translate real-world control problems into optimization models that minimizes customers’ energy bills.
* Develop and improve forecasting and decision-support models enabling optimal control schedules for customers’ home energy devices.
* Design and run experiments on real customer systems, evaluate impact, and turn insights into product decisions.
* Work closely with the team to bring models into production.
* Work across the full data stack, including data pipelines, feature engineering, deployment and monitoring.
* Lead initiatives from discovery to production impact. You set direction, align with stakeholders across product and engineering to deliver outcomes even when the path is not fully defined.
* Communicate clearly to technical and non-technical audiences, explaining trade-offs, uncertainty, and what is good enough to ship.


**What You Bring To The Team**
* Several years of experience as a Data Scientist, Optimization Engineer, Applied Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer or similar.
* Strong mathematical and statistical modelling skills.
* Experience with time-series forecasting and machine learning, ideally applied to operational systems.
* Experience working in Python and with modern ML/optimization tooling and workflows.
* Solid Software Engineering fundamentals for deploying and maintaining ML systems in production.
* A pragmatic, product-oriented mindset: you optimize for real-world impact, not model complexity.
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).


**Nice to have**
* Experience with constrained optimization and applying it to real-world systems.
* Experience with energy systems or electricity market knowledge, especially flexibility and demand response.
* Experience with control system or physical systems modelling.


**What is in it for you?**
* Gain hands-on experience in a fast-growing scale-up, with a supportive and inclusive culture, and career advancement opportunities.
* Collaborate with diverse teams across international markets and launch products globally.
* Develop and grow personally and professionally in an entrepreneurial-minded environment.
* Thrive in a dynamic, solution-oriented culture, tackling challenges and driving meaningful solutions that make a positive impact on the world.
* Influence the company's success, with a visible impact on product development and market growth.


 At Aira, We’re not just champions of next-generation energy, we’re champions in Diversity and Inclusion too. We believe that a diverse workforce sparks innovation and creativity and enables us to better serve our customers and communities. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. We will never accept any form of discrimination and believe that together, we can drive a new era for our people and planet.

title: Data & AI Platform Engineer – Technical Lead
description:
*Do you want to lead the development of platforms that power next-generation AI solutions?*
*At Saab, we are expanding our capabilities in Data & AI platforms and are looking for a
 **Technical Lead** 
 to join the Software Excellence and Information Assurance (SWEIXA) team within the Surveillance business area.*
 In this senior role, you will combine technical leadership with hands-on engineering, guiding the design and implementation of cloud-native Data & AI platforms. You will play a key role in shaping our AI solutions, working closely with both engineers and stakeholders to ensure our platforms are secure, scalable and aligned with customer needs.
   

  

 You will also contribute with your expertise in AI tooling, its possibilities and limitations, and support the team in building robust environments for advanced AI solutions.
   

  

 Occasional travel may be required.
   

  

 Your Role
   

  

 As a Technical Lead, you will guide the architecture and development of our Data & AI platforms while also supporting the team with technical expertise and mentorship.
   

  

 Your responsibilities will include:
   

  

* Designing the end-to-end architecture and technical implementation of our platforms
* Leading the development of complete AI systems and platform capabilities
* Understanding customer needs and translating business requirements into deployable architecture
* Taking the lead in deploying solutions, including setup, configuration and troubleshooting
* Ensuring efficient operations, observability and performance of Data & AI environments
* Creating guidelines and best practices for AI platform management
* Providing technical leadership and mentoring to team members
* Supporting the team with technical troubleshooting and problem solving
* Staying up to date with AI technologies and trends, contributing to the product roadmap


 Your Profile
   

  

 We are looking for a senior engineer who enjoys combining technical leadership, architecture and hands-on development. You are comfortable working with complex systems, large data environments and modern cloud-native technologies.
   

  

 You likely have experience with several of the following:
   

  

* Strong experience in Golang or other systems programming languages
* Implementation of Kubernetes operators
* Cloud architecture and cloud-native platforms
* DevOps practices including GitOps workflows
* Automation of repetitive tasks using Shell or Python
* Linux environments and container technologies
* CI/CD pipelines using tools such as ArgoCD, Argo Workflows or GitHub Actions
* Security practices such as scanning third-party libraries and container images
* Building Kubernetes environments in data-intensive industries
* Experience with AI model deployment
* Experience working with AI-assisted development or agentic AI


 You also have a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a technical field such as Data Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent experience.
   

  

 Experience in any of the following areas is considered a plus:
   

  

* Build and testing pipelines including unit, integration and end-to-end testing
* Security testing such as SAST and DAST
* LLM concepts such as prompting and RAG
* AI certifications (Google, AWS or Microsoft)
* NVIDIA developer certification
* Agile methodologies such as SAFe


 What we offer
   

  

* A dynamic and international work environment
* Opportunities for professional growth and development
* A competitive salary and benefits package
* The chance to work with cutting-edge AI technologies


 What You Will Be Part Of
   

  

 Here you have the opportunity to influence and contribute in your own unique way, and every contribution counts. We give you the opportunity to take on challenges and create smart innovations. In our friendly and tech-savvy world, there is space for you to grow. We are here to create safety and security for people and societies together.
   

  

 Saab is a leading defense and security company with a mission to help nations protect their populations and contribute to safety for people and societies. With 23,000 talented employees, Saab develops technology and solutions for a safer and more sustainable world.
   

  

 Saab develops, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control systems, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden but operates globally and is part of many nations’ defense capabilities.

title: Microsoft Security Consultant
description:
**Summary**
 At Avanade, you will support our customers in understanding security risks and improving their data protection solutions. You’ll work across both Avanade and Accenture clients, ranging from medium to large global enterprises. You’ll be part of a collaborative and diverse team that values learning, knowledge sharing, and practical problem solving – helping you to grow confidence and capability as a security consultant. We look for consultants who are curious, proactive, and comfortable challenging ideas – while continuing to learn and grow their expertise.
   

  

 If you already are a passionate security consultant and want to take the next step in a consultant role where you can shape your future, broaden your impact and grow into more complex responsibilities – this is the opportunity for you.
   

  

 We believe you can be our next innovator, risk-taker, and challenger of the status quo at Avanade.
   

  

**Come join us**
 As a member of Avanade’s security practice, you will be in a unique position to work for both Avanade and Accenture clients, supporting security and data protection initiatives for mid-size organizations and large global enterprises. You will contribute directly to client engagements, working hands-on with Microsoft security technologies while collaborating closely with experienced consultants.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 As a member of Avanade’s security practice, you will be in a unique position to work for both Avanade and Accenture clients, supporting security and data protection initiatives for mid-size organizations and large global enterprises. You will contribute directly to client engagements, working hands-on with Microsoft security technologies while collaborating closely with experienced consultants.
   

  

 You’re a team-oriented security consultant with broad Microsoft 365 understanding especially in security and compliance products. You enjoy working with technologies like Purview, Entra, Defender and Sentinel, and hopefully some non-Microsoft tools. You like working with others, value different perspectives, and are comfortable asking questions to deepen your understanding and grow your skills.
   

  

**What You’ll Do In This Role**
* Design and deliver Microsoftbased security and data protection solutions
* Support customer workshops, assessments, and solution discussions, with expectations to lead as you grow in the role
* Take an active role in knowledge sharing, continuous improvement, and evolving how we deliver security services
* Stay current with Microsoft security technologies and partner solutions through training, certification, and handson project work
* Proactively suggest improvements, challenge approaches when appropriate, and take responsibility for your own development
* Take an active role at partner events and customer meetings


 This role is designed for consultants who already have practical experience and are eager to take the next step — gaining broader exposure, increase responsibility, and the opportunity to grow into more complex and impactful security engagements.
   

  

**Qualifications**
**Required Skills & Experience:**
* Proven experience with Microsoft Security technologies including Microsoft Purview, Defender, Sentinel, and Entra ID.
* Hands-on experience with DLP policy design, migration and refinement
* Strong understanding of data protection regulations (e.g. GDPR, DPA 2018, ISO 27001, NIST, EU AI Act).
* Familiarity with AI security concepts, including DSPM, model governance, and data lineage.
* Understanding of Zero Trust architecture and cloud-native security controls.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
* Relevant certifications (e.g., SC-400, SC-200, AZ-500, CISSP, CIPP/E, AI-specific certs) are highly desirable,


**Skills**
 Ideally, you would have had experience working on the Microsoft platform, however we can provide focused training to the right candidates who can demonstrate their passion, experience and thought leadership in this space. To hit the ground running, your skills may include:
   

  

* Proven experience of Microsoft Information Protection and with data classification infrastructures and techniques to classifying unstructured and structured data both on-premises and in the cloud.
* Proven experience of Data Loss Prevention technologies across endpoints, email and the internet, including Microsoft Purview.
* Proven experience securing Microsoft 365, leveraging Microsoft Secure Score with a deep understanding of the security capabilities of Microsoft 365.
* Proven experience in techniques and approaches towards the protection of data stored in databases and applications.
* Deep expertise in data confidentiality and data integrity principles.
* Deep expertise of business, privacy, security, and compliance challenges su-rounding Data Protection, including being able to research and understand data privacy requirements.
* A keen interest in Cyber Security, maintaining a good understanding of capabilities across the industry, and in developing their own Cyber experience to continue progression.
* With an eye to the future, you are aware of emerging technologies in the Data Protection space.


**Characteristics That Can Spell Success For This Role**
* Experience in consulting and advisory discussions with clients and other consulting firms.
* Experience in owning the design and implementation of technology solutions.
* You are a Subject matter expert, who has experience working on the Microsoft platform.
* Experience managing deliverables and being responsible for delivering against critical milestones.
* Ability to collaborate within a large global organization.
* You have implemented work plans for assigned projects, programs and clients against established operational objective.
* You are seen as a role model and act as a leader.


**Some Of The Best Things About Working At Avanade**
* Opportunity to work for Microsoft’s Global Alliance Partner of the Year


 As you bring your skills and abilities to Avanade, you’ll get distinctive experiences, limitless learning, and ambitious growth in return. As we continue to build our diverse and inclusive culture, we become even more innovative and creative, helping us better serve our clients and communities. You’ll join a community of smart, supportive collaborators to lift, mentor, and guide you, and to lean on your expertise. You get a company purpose-built for business-critical, leading-edge technology solutions, committed to improving the way humans work, interact, and live. It’s all here, so take a closer look!
   

  

**Together We Do What Matters.**
 We work hard to provide an inclusive, diverse culture with a deep sense of belonging for all our employees. Visit our Inclusion & Diversity page.
   

  

 Create a future for our people that focuses on
   

  

* Expanding your thinking
* Experimenting courageously
* Learning and pivoting


 Inspire greatness in our people by
   

  

* Empowering every voice
* Encouraging boldness
* Celebrating progress


 Accelerate the impact of our people by
   

  

* Amazing the client
* Prioritizing what matters
* Acting as one

title: Solution Architect
description:
Are you a dynamic and experienced Solution Architect with a passion for collaborating towards success in a high-tech, innovative environment? Join our forward-thinking Solution Architect team at ASSA ABLOY Global Solutions, based in Stockholm, Sweden. You'll be part of a collaborative culture that empowers you to build a career you can be proud of.
   

  

**What You Would Do As Our Solution Architect**
 You would be responsible for designing and implementing scalable, secure, and reliable solution architectures to address our evolving business needs. This role involves close collaboration with development teams, system architects, vendors, and stakeholders to ensure the successful delivery of projects.
   

  

 You would also:
   

  

* Develop end-to-end IoT solution architectures that address current and future business requirements, including data collection from sensors, equipment, and appliances.
* Support the teams in designing and implementing cutting-edge technology solutions that align with ASSA ABLOY's business goals and objectives.
* Assess existing systems and technologies to identify areas for improvement and provide technical expertise and recommendations to stakeholders.
* Monitor emerging technology trends and proactively suggest innovative solutions to enhance our capabilities.
* Conduct technical due diligence to support the M&A team in evaluating potential acquisitions.


**The Skills And Experience You Need**
 We are looking for someone who has:
   

  

* At least 5 years of relevant experience as a Solution Architect in industrial products, IoT cloud environments, data and analytics architectures, or a similar field.
* Proven expertise in designing and implementing scalable, secure solutions in cloud environments, with hands-on experience in IoT-based wireless technologies such as ZigBee, BLE, and similar protocols.
* A strong understanding of software development life cycle (SDLC) methodologies.
* Good understanding of IT security including cryptography and preferably also PKI infrastructure
* Excellent communication skills to collaborate effectively with stakeholders and the ability to lead cross-functional teams to deliver projects on time and budget.
* A bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.


 Ideally, you also have relevant certifications in AWS or Azure or have experience in M&A and in the hospitality industry.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a successful career with us – here’s what we have to offer:
   

  

* Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills.
* A competitive salary and incentive schemes.
* The opportunity to grow your skills in a versatile way and develop your career in a respected global group.
* Agile and innovative working environment.
* Motivated and encouraging team around you.
* Stable employment in a friendly international atmosphere.


**We review applications regularly, so don’t wait**
 We are building diverse, inclusive teams, and encourage applications from everyone who can see themselves working with us. Just set up your profile and apply.
   

  

 To make sure your personal data is safe, we don’t look at any applications sent by email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the recruitment process, please email Alexandra Kymäläinen, Talent Acquisition Business Partner, at alexandra.kymalainen@assaabloy.com.
   

  

 Depending on the risk profile for the role, a background screening will need to be performed. Final candidates may therefore be a subject to background screening. The performance of a background check is subject to consent and based on our Applicant Privacy Notice.
   

  

 Let’s create a safer and more open world - together!
   

  

 To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com
   

  

**We are the ASSA ABLOY Group**
 Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 61,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access.
   

  

 As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally.
   

  

 As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Senior Software Engineer – Tieto Banktech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
 Tieto Banktech powers some of the Nordics’ most trusted banking systems with secure, reliable, and scalable solutions. Using automation, data, AI, and cloud, supported by hands-on guidance, we help banks modernize their core, strengthen trust, and deliver greater impact across the banking experience.
   

  

 We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer who is both hands-on and strategic, to play a pivotal role in modernizing and leading the development of our Equity post-trade platform. Working closely with the Product Manager, you will spend the majority of your time coding while shaping the architecture, mentoring the team, and driving the transition from legacy monolithic systems to modular, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Hands-on Development: Architect, design, and implement robust solutions, with a focus on RESTful APIs, microservices, and domain-driven design.
* Legacy Modernization: Lead the refactoring of monolithic, DB-driven systems, lifting business logic out of stored procedures into modular and maintainable components.
* Technical Leadership: Mentor and guide a distributed engineering team (Stockholm & Pune), promoting best practices, clean code, and technical excellence.
* Architecture Strategy: Collaborate with Product Manager to define and implement a technical roadmap aligned with business goals.
* Quality & Resilience: Drive initiatives to improve code quality, reliability, and maintainability in complex systems.
* Business Collaboration: Translate complex business workflows into technical solutions while balancing speed, scalability, and risk.


**Required Skills & Competencies**
* Strong programming experience in SQL, C#, and .NET, with a track record of hands-on coding in production systems.
* Proven experience in API design, microservices architecture, and domain-driven design.
* Track record of driving technical transformation in legacy systems.
* Demonstrated ability to modernize legacy, DB-heavy systems, refactor monolithic codebases, and implement modular architectures.
* Experience leading and mentoring distributed teams, fostering knowledge sharing and technical growth.
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, cloud platforms (Azure), Git, and DevOps practices.
* Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
* Experience in Equity post-trade or Capital Markets systems.
* Understanding of equity trading workflows and complex business scenarios.
* Minimum 7 years of experience working on enterprise-level solutions.
* Hands-on experience with containerization technologies (Docker/Kubernetes) and cloud-native architectures.
* Experience working in a product organization with strong collaboration between tech and business.


**We Offer**
 We offer a unique opportunity to shape the architecture of a critical banking platform, work on meaningful projects, and influence a growing, distributed team. You will have the freedom to be hands-on while also driving modernization, supported by an open culture, flexible hybrid work model, and a focus on continuous learning.
   

  

 Interested?
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application! Interviews will be held continuously, so we encourage you to apply today. For more information or questions, please contact our Talent Acquisition team at recruitmentinbanking@tieto.com.
   

  

 We perform background checks on all final candidates.
   

  

 Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.
   

  

 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.
   

  

 Diversity, equity and inclusion (tietoevry.com)

title: Technical Product Manager
description:
**About Axiomatics** 




 At Axiomatics, we develop world-leading solutions for
 **Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)** 
 that protect critical data for banks, government agencies, and global enterprises. Here, you will work with cutting-edge technology and complex security solutions.
 



 Founded in 2006, Axiomatics is a leading provider of dynamic authorization and fine-grained access control. As a subsidiary of
 **Leonardo SpA** 
 —a global leader in Aerospace, Defence, and Security—we combine the agility of a tech leader with the robust governance of a major international group.
 



 Learn more at www.axiomatics.com.
 



  





**Work description** 




 As a Technical Product Manager at Axiomatics, you will:
 


* Drive the execution of strategically important initiatives within Axiomatics’ authorization platform in a hands-on, technically oriented role
* Own the end-to-end execution of prioritized product areas, from requirements definition and technical alignment to delivery and follow-up
* Lead complex initiatives by breaking them down into clear, structured, and deliverable steps, defining scope, milestones, and dependencies with Engineering and Architecture
* Prioritize and manage product backlogs in line with the roadmap and delivery capacity, ensuring realistic, transparent, and technically feasible commitments
* Act as the primary product counterpart and Product Owner towards Engineering and Architecture, ensuring high-quality requirements, delivery readiness, and effective decision-making
* Translate platform and architectural capabilities into concrete product features and clear customer value, working at significant technical depth (APIs, integrations, policy models, enforcement points, complex data flows)
* Collaborate cross-functionally with UX, Architecture, Platform, Field, Sales, and Customer Success teams to deliver usable, sustainable, and long-term solutions
* Communicate clearly with internal stakeholders on delivery status, risks, dependencies, and trade-offs, and contribute actively to roadmap and prioritization discussions
* Engage closely with customers to validate requirements, use cases, and technical constraints, and support demos, workshops, and roadmap discussions where technical credibility is essential



  





**Your profile** 




 We are looking for someone with an academic background in Data Engineering, Information systems, Information security, or a related field, combined with several years of experience in Product Management within Enterprise B2B SaaS, preferably focusing on platforms and security products. You have a strong technical background and experience working with platform products, and you are accustomed to close collaboration with Engineering to translating complex technical concepts into clear product requirements. You are highly communicative in English and comfortable working with agile methodologies such as Scrum and Kanban, as well as in globally distributed teams and complex, fast-changing environments.
 



 Experience in Identity & Access Management (IAM), authorization, RBAC/ABAC, policy as code, and the PEP–PDP model is considered a strong advantage, as is experience supporting enterprise customers with complex technical requirements. Knowledge of APIs, developer platforms, or infrastructure products, as well as experience with AI use cases and scaling, is also highly valued.
 



 As a person, you are a clear, confident, and analytical communicator. You are structured, self-driven, and accountable, with a curious, innovative, and solution-oriented mindset. You are motivated by technical challenges and by creating real customer value.
 



  





**Axiomatics offers** 




 At Axiomatics, you will have the opportunity to work with advanced technology in fine-grained access control and be part of solutions used by large global enterprises and government organizations that rely on our products to protect their most critical assets. You will join an inspiring, technically strong environment characterized by trust, curiosity, and technical excellence, where continuous learning and professional development are a natural part of everyday work. You will also have the opportunity to influence, take ownership, and contribute directly to our customers’ success in their security journeys.
 



  





**Apply today!** 




 For this recruitment, we are collaborating with Inhouse IT & Tech. This is a full-time, permanent position based at our office in central Stockholm.

title: Konsult Microsoft Dynamics 365
description:
På Sogeti tror vi att det bästa finns inom oss alla. Oavsett om du är i början av din karriär eller redan på toppen av ditt spel, kommer vi att uppmuntra dig att nå din fulla potential och bli bättre. Genom vår gemensamma passion för teknik, vår entreprenöriella kultur och vårt fokus på kontinuerligt lärande, kommer vi att ge dig allt du behöver för att göra ditt bästa arbete och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.
   

  

**Konsult Dynamics 365**
**Om rollen**
 Accelerera din karriär och bli nästa generations konsult inom Microsoft Dynamics 365 för Finance och Supply Chain (AX) & Microsoft Dynamics Customer Engagement (CRM)! Som konsult hos Sogeti kommer du att stötta några av de största, mest älskade varumärkena i Europa på deras digitala transformationsresa.
   

  

*I den här rollen kommer du att ha en nyckelroll i:*
* Att arbeta med Microsoft Dynamics, antingen som konsult, superanvändare, processägare eller i samband med implementeringsprojekt.
* Att briljera i rollen som konsult och stötta kunder på deras digitala transformationsresa.
* Att använda din erfarenhet av processkunskap inom finance eller manufacturing för att leverera högkvalitativa lösningar.
* Att bidra till utveckling och implementering av ERP/CRM-system.


**Din profil**
* 2-3 års relevant arbetslivserfarenhet.
* Erfarenhet av processkunskap inom finance eller manufacturing.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med Microsoft Dynamics eller annan ERP/CRM-plattform, antingen som konsult, superanvändare, processägare eller i liknande roll.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i samband med en ERP/CRM-implementering.
* Pratar och skriver svenska obehindrat.


**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas:**
* Innovativ miljö: Hos oss får du chansen att jobba med den senaste tekniken och vara en del av ett dynamiskt team som älskar att skapa värde genom innovation.
* Karriärutveckling: Vi bryr oss om din framtid och erbjuder utvecklingsprogram och certifieringar i de senaste teknologierna, så att du kan växa och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.
* Flexibelt arbete: Vi förstår att livet är mer än bara jobb. Därför erbjuder vi möjligheter till distansarbete och flexibla arbetstider för att du ska må bra och trivas.
* Teamkänsla: Vi är som en stor familj med en härlig teamkänsla. Genom att leva efter våra värdeord som mod, passion, laganda och entreprenörskap - skapar vi en arbetsmiljö där alla kan trivas.


**Ansökan**
*Varmt välkommen med din ansökan idag, urval sker löpande! Hoppas vi ses! Vill du veta mer? Kontakta TA Partner Marta Sundell så berättar hon gärna mer.*
*Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov.*
 Om Sogeti
   

  

 Sogeti Sverige, en del av Capgemini-koncernen, med 19 kontor och 1200 medarbetare, skapar affärsvärde med teknologi för organisationer som behöver införa innovation snabbt och som vill ha en lokal partner med global skalbarhet. Med en hands-on-kultur och närhet till kunderna, implementerar Sogeti lösningar som hjälper organisationer att arbeta snabbare, bättre och smartare. Genom att kombinera agilitet och snabb implementation via en DevOps-ansats, levererar Sogeti innovativa lösningar inom test- och kvalitetssäkring, molnet och systemutveckling, förstärkta av AI, data och automation.

title: Staff Mobile Engineer: Instrumentation SDK (iOS/Android) - Observability | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies -- including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay -- manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo).
   

  

 We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.
   

  

 You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.
   

  

**This is a remote opportunity and we would be interested in applicants based in Spain, Germany, the UK or Sweden at this time.**
**The Opportunity**
 Grafana Observability builds end-to-end observability that spans application, infrastructure, database, browser, and mobile. Our Mobile Observability initiative gives mobile developers first-class instrumentation SDKs that capture traces, metrics, logs, crashes, and user sessions. Our solutions aim to be tightly integrated with OpenTelemetry and Grafana Cloud. We’re building high-performance, low-overhead SDKs that make it easy for teams to connect mobile signals to backend and infra telemetry for true full-stack visibility.
   

  

 We value open standards, great developer experience, and doing the hard engineering needed to ship reliable software at scale. You may not meet every requirement below. If this role excites you, please raise your hand.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Build and evolve mobile instrumentation SDKs for iOS (Swift / Obj-C) and Android (Kotlin / Java), prioritizing performance, stability, and a great developer experience.
* Design and ship SDK features such as session tracking, crash reporting, sampling strategies, automatic context propagation, and OpenTelemetry integrations.
* Own projects end-to-end.
* Break large, ambiguous problems into incremental deliverables: design modular solutions, ship MVPs, gather feedback, iterate quickly.
* Ensure quality: automated tests, CI, documentation, and smooth, backwards-compatible upgrade paths.
* Collaborate cross-functionally with backend, frontend, product, and solutions engineering to ensure cohesive end-to-end observability workflows.
* Engage with the open-source community: review PRs, discuss design proposals, and help guide external contributors.
* Mentor teammates, participate in technical design discussions and help shape the mobile observability roadmap.


 We invest heavily in developer productivity. You can use modern AI coding assistants as part of your daily workflow (your choice of tools, within security guidelines), backed by a company-funded usage budget so you can iterate quickly without unnecessary friction.
   

  

 We encourage pragmatic AI-assisted development: faster prototyping, test generation, refactors, documentation, and incident follow-ups—always paired with strong code review and quality standards.
   

  

 You’ll also have access to frontier models (e.g., GPT-Codex 5/3, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro).
   

  

**What Makes You a Great Fit**
* You’re a motivated self-starter with a bias toward action and the ability to run with poorly defined scopes. You can operate autonomously while being highly collaborative.
* Strong mobile engineering experience (one or both): iOS (Swift/Obj-C) and/or Android (Kotlin/Java).
* Demonstrated experience building or maintaining mobile SDKs, platform libraries, or other developer-facing platform code used by external developers.
* Experience working with performance-sensitive code (startup time, memory, network efficiency) and making pragmatic tradeoffs.
* Familiarity with OpenTelemetry (instrumentation, SDKs, collectors) or experience contributing to/maintaining OTel SDKs or similar telemetry libraries.
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* Comfortable working in a fully remote, globally distributed team.
* Coachable, customer-focused, and passionate about developer experience, you can do more than code: you write docs, mentor others, and shape roadmaps.
* Pragmatic and comfortable navigating ambiguity: you prioritize progress over perfection and iterate quickly based on feedback.


**Bonus Points For**
* Contributions to OpenTelemetry, crash reporting libraries, or other observability OSS.
* Experience with cross-platform frameworks (React Native, Flutter) or building native bindings/bridges.
* Experience with mobile security, privacy, or platform integration challenges.
* Prior work on crash aggregation, session replay, or sampling for mobile telemetry.


 In Sweden, the compensation range for this role is SEK 878,578 - SEK 1,054,294. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed throughout the interview process. All of our roles include Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), giving every team member ownership in Grafana Labs' success. We believe in shared outcomes—RSUs help us stay aligned and invested as we scale globally.
   

  

* Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.


**Why You’ll Thrive At Grafana Labs**
* 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. \*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** 
 We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. We believe that equality and diversity builds a strong organization and we’re working hard to make sure that’s the foundation of our organization as we grow.
   

  

*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*
*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our privacy policy.*

title: LINUX ENGINEER TILL BANKGIROT
description:
*Bankgirot söker en Linux Engineer till en växande del av organisationen i Stockholm.* 
*Bankgirot har en unik position på Sveriges betalmarknad, som just nu genomgår sin största transformation i modern tid.* 
*Vår IT-organisation växer för att utveckla framtidssäkra lösningar som uppfyller internationella standarder och regelverk.
   

  

 Samtidigt säkerställer vi att det nuvarande betalsystemet fungerar stabilt och säkert – varje dag.* 
*Utvecklingen av framtidens betalningsinfrastruktur är i full gång och vi söker dig som vill vara en del av resan. Som
 *Linux* 
 Engineer får du en nyckelroll i att säkra framtidens betalningslösningar på ett teknikbolag där vi brinner för vårt uppdrag – och för varandra.*
 Din roll
   

  

 Det här är en spännande möjlighet för dig som vill vara med och bygga upp en helt ny och modern infrastruktur. Du blir en del av ett växande Linuxteam som just nu formas, där du tillsammans med kollegor och vår Tech Lead får en nyckelroll i att både drifta och vidareutveckla vår nya Greenfield-miljö.
   

  

 Rollen är bred och varierad – du arbetar med allt från daglig drift och felsökning till automatisering, säkerhetshärdning och applikationsdrift. Du får stort ansvar för Linuxplattformen och arbetar proaktivt med att effektivisera miljöer, införa nya lösningar och bidra till hög tillgänglighet och säkerhet. Du får också möjlighet att påverka tekniska vägval, etablera rutiner och vara med och bygga långsiktig kompetens inom området – i en organisation med ett högt säkerhetsfokus och ett samhällsviktigt uppdrag.
   

  

**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden inkluderar:**
* Driva och vidareutveckla vår nya Linuxinfrastruktur med fokus på tillgänglighet, säkerhet och effektivitet
* Ansvara för drift, underhåll och övervakning av servrar, användare och applikationer
* Utföra felsökning och logganalys för att säkerställa stabil och säker drift
* Automatisera och standardisera miljöer med Bash och Ansible
* Säkerställa korrekt konfiguration, behörigheter och härdning av system
* Hantera applikationsdrift för exempelvis webb- och e-posttjänster i Linuxmiljö


 Vi söker dig som har
   

  

 För att lyckas i rollen har du goda kunskaper inom Linuxadministration och är trygg i att hantera drift av servrar, användarhantering och systemresurser. Du har arbetat med felsökning, övervakning och logganalys, och har en god förståelse för hur man bygger stabila, säkra och effektiva miljöer.
   

  

 Skriptning och automatisering är en självklar del av din verktygslåda – gärna med Bash och Ansible – och har du också erfarenhet av att arbeta med virtualiseringslösningar som VMware och Linuxbaserade VDI-miljöer är detta ett plus. Du är noggrann med säkerhet, rättigheter och härdning av system, och du delar gärna med dig av din kunskap och dokumenterar det du bygger.
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du också har erfarenhet av databashantering, ett intresse för DevOps och cloud-native-arkitekturer, eller har arbetat i miljöer med höga krav på tillgänglighet och kontinuitet. Viktigast är att du har ett driv att lära dig och att du vill bidra till att bygga något långsiktigt.
   

  

 Oavsett om du är erfaren eller i början av din Linuxresa söker vi dig som har ett genuint teknikintresse, som gärna labbar på egen hand och som vill förstå systemen på djupet – inte bara få dem att fungera. Här får du möjlighet att växa tillsammans med ett engagerat team som bygger framtidens plattform – på riktigt.
   

  

 Ett utvecklande arbete med bra förmåner
   

  

 Som vår nya kollega blir du en del av ett kompetent och positivt team där vi samarbetar och sätter högt värde på varandras kompetens och erfarenheter.
   

  

 Vi satsar mycket på hälsa och välmående och erbjuder generösa förmåner för dig som medarbetare.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning baserad i Liljeholmen i Stockholm. Du behöver ha goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska.
   

  

 Innan anställning beslutas genomförs en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585).
   

  

 Intresserad?
   

  

 I denna rekrytering samarbetar Bankgirot med Nexer Recruit.
   

  

 Har du frågor eller funderingar? Kontakta gärna rekryteringskonsult Johanna Värmfors: johanna.varmfors@nexergroup.com eller +46 730 821 230 alternativt Jenny Nilsson: jenny.nilsson@nexergroup.com eller +46 703 018 279.
   

  

 Läs om fler lediga tjänster från Bankgirot här.
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
   

  

 Om Bankgirot
   

  

 Bankgirot är ett svenskt clearinghus och teknikbolag med över 60 års erfarenhet av att leverera finansiell infrastruktur till banker. I samverkan med betalsektorn ser vi till att privatpersoner och företag enkelt kan göra överföringar och betalningar.
   

  

 Bankgirot säkerställer att det nuvarande betalsystemet fungerar felfritt, samtidigt som vi utvecklar framtidssäkrade lösningar. Vi säkrar och utvecklar Sveriges betalningar.
   

  

 Läs mer på www.bankgirot.se

title: Senior Performance Marketer
description:
**Paligo** 
 is the leading cloud-based component content management system (CCMS) for technical documentation. We help teams create, manage, translate, and publish structured content at scale, with 450+ customers across 38 countries.
   

  

 This role is based in Stockholm and reports to the VP of Marketing.
   

  

**Role Overview**
 Search is changing fast. AI Mode, zero-click results, and LLM-driven research are rewriting the rules of B2B discovery. We're looking for a
 **Senior Performance Marketer** 
 who sees this shift as an opportunity. Someone who can own our traffic acquisition, optimize conversions on our site, and make sure Paligo shows up wherever our buyers are looking.
   

  

 This role owns the full spectrum: organic, paid and AI search, affiliate partnerships, and paligo.net itself. You'll drive targeted traffic, optimize how visitors convert, and continuously test and iterate to fuel a high-velocity sales pipeline.
   

  

 This is both strategic and hands-on. You'll shape our SEO and SEM capabilities for today's rapidly evolving search landscape while also building the site, managing budgets, and measuring what moves the needle, every single day. At Paligo all team members roll up their sleeves and do the work. You'll collaborate closely with our Marketing Manager, Content Writer, Revenue Operations team, and Digital Designer.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Traffic acquisition**
* Build and optimize our organic search program, including technical SEO, content strategy, and authority building to improve rankings and capture high-intent traffic.
* Develop and execute our AI search visibility strategy. Research and optimize how Paligo is cited and recommended through Google, Claude, ChatGPt etc.
* Run paid search campaigns across Google and Microsoft Ads. Manage budgets, optimize bids, and drive efficient cost-per-lead.
* Manage and develop affiliate partnerships with industry influencers and organizations.


**Website & conversion optimization**
* Own Paligo's website end to end. Build and optimize pages, improve conversion rates, and continuously test and iterate to turn traffic into Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs).
* Shape the on-site experience in collaboration with Digital Design, from landing pages to CTAs over the entire visitor journey.
* Measure what matters: report on performance across all channels, build attribution models, and use data to prioritize where to invest next.


**Growth opportunities**
* For the right person: take ownership of our ABM program and level up how marketing supports outbound.
* Experiment with emerging channels and formats as the search landscape evolves. This role has the mandate to test, learn, and scale what works.
* Shape Paligo's approach to AI-driven discovery as a competitive advantage. This is a space where best practices are still being written.


**What You Bring**
**Must-haves**
* A genuine curiosity about how AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini are reshaping how B2B buyers discover and evaluate software, and a drive to turn that understanding into competitive advantage.
* 7+ years of hands-on digital marketing experience in B2B SaaS, including proven results in driving traffic growth and improving conversion rates.
* Expertise in SEO and SEM (Google Ads, Microsoft Ads) for B2B audiences.
* Strong analytical and action-oriented mindset. Comfortable working with Google Analytics, Search Console, HubSpot reporting, and campaign attribution data to make decisions.
* Working proficiency in WordPress (or similar CMS) for building and optimizing web pages.


**Nice-to-haves**
* Experience with account-based marketing (ABM) programs.
* Familiarity with the creation of technical documentation, knowledge or content management.
* Experience working with agencies or freelancers to scale marketing output.


**What You'll Focus On (and Be Measured On)**
* Volume and quality of Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs) generated across all channels and the resulting business opportunities.
* Paligo's visibility and citations in AI-driven search.
* Traffic growth across organic, paid, and AI-driven channels, and the efficiency of spend behind it.
* Website conversion rates and the quality of the on-site visitor experience.


**Why join us**
 At Paligo, your work directly shapes how a global SaaS company grows. You'll have real ownership, visible impact, and the support to do your best work.
   

  

* Own the full traffic and conversion engine for a company with strong product-market fit and room to grow.
* Shape how Paligo shows up in the next generation of search, including AI, LLMs, and beyond.
* A collaborative, remote-friendly culture grounded in ownership, curiosity, and real impact.
* Competitive compensation and benefits.


**About Paligo**
 Paligo was founded in 2015 and is an end-to-end Component Content Management System (CCMS) trusted by global organizations to create, manage, and deliver technical documentation, policies & procedures, and knowledge management content. Our customers include global enterprises like Emerson Electric, Washington Post, Alteryx, and Sitecore. Our mission is to empower teams to create, collaborate, and elevate, turning knowledge into an asset anywhere and anytime.
   

  

**Perks & Benefits**
* Flexible working hours, remote work / home office support
* Competitive pension, insurance (sick, life, occupational injury)
* Wellness allowance + gym at the Stockholm office
* Parental leave pay contribution (including pension benefits in first three months)
* Development opportunities: internal & external workshops, education support
* Social perks: breakfast, "fika" (Swedish coffee culture) and great colleagues!


 Paligo is an end-to-end Component Content Management System (CCMS) solution for technical documentation, policies and procedures, knowledge management, and more.

title: AI Lead Engineer
description:
**The opportunity**
 Hitachi Energy is establishing an AI Center of Competence (CoC) to accelerate engineering productivity, improve design quality, and enable next-generation intelligent engineering solutions across civil, mechanical, electrical, controls, and software disciplines.
   

  

 The AI Lead Engineer will shape the vision, establish foundational capabilities (governance, platforms, operating model), and deliver early AI pilots that create tangible value for engineers and project teams.
   

  

 This is a hands-on leadership role combining technical depth, domain understanding, and strong stakeholder engagement.
   

  

**How You’ll Make An Impact**
* Establish the AI Center of Competence from the ground up Define the mission, operating model, governance framework, and technical standards for responsible AI adoption. Build trusted partnerships with IT, cybersecurity, legal, and engineering leadership to ensure scalable, secure, and compliant AI deployment across disciplines.
* Deliver measurable engineering productivity gains within the first year Identify and execute 2–3 high impact AI pilots (e.g., engineering documentation automation, design knowledge retrieval, controls/code assistance) that demonstrably reduce engineering cycle time, improve quality, and free up expert capacity.
* Translate engineering pain points into deployable AI solutions Work hands-on with civil, mechanical, electrical, controls, and software teams to deeply understand workflows, tools (PLM, CAD, ERP, document systems), and constraints, turning real world challenges into practical, high value AI applications.
* Build AI-ready data foundations for long term scale Enable structured, governed, and discoverable engineering data by advancing data ownership, metadata standards, lineage tracking, and access control, laying the groundwork for advanced capabilities such as knowledge graphs and intelligent agents.
* Bridge strategy and execution in industrial AI adoption Act as a trusted advisor to engineering leadership, clearly articulating AI opportunities, risks, and ROI while personally leading prototyping, experimentation, and early deployment in enterprise environments.
* Shape the future of intelligent engineering at scale Define the roadmap beyond initial pilots, including AI assisted design reviews, engineering copilots, and cross disciplinary knowledge systems.


**Your background**
* Strong expertise in applied AI and machine learning Proven experience designing and deploying AI solutions in enterprise environments, with particular depth in Generative AI, LLMs, NLP, and document intelligence.
* Experience building production grade AI systems Familiarity with the full AI lifecycle including data pipelines, model development, MLOps practices, and deployment on modern cloud platforms.
* Understanding of engineering or industrial environments Exposure to engineering heavy domains such as power systems, mechanical, electrical, controls, automation, or similar industrial contexts.
* Experience working with complex engineering systems and data Familiarity with tools and enterprise systems such as PLM, ERP, CAD/CAE, requirements management, or large scale document management environments.
* Strong stakeholder leadership and influence Demonstrated ability to collaborate across engineering, IT, and leadership teams, translating technical AI concepts into practical value for non-AI specialists.
* Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to build from zero Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments, establishing new capabilities, shaping strategy, and driving adoption of emerging technologies across organizations.


**What We Offer**
* Collective agreement
* Flexible working time
* Health care and wellness allowance
* Fantastic career possibilities within Hitachi Energy both within Sweden and globally
* Mentor to support you throughout onboard phase
* Various trainings and education supporting employee development
* Diversified company with over 70+ nationalities working in Sweden
* Supplementary compensation for parental leave
* Employee Benefit Portal with thousands of discounts and perks


**More About Us**
 Are you ready for a new exciting challenge? Does the above description sound like you? Welcome to apply! Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don’t delay – apply today!
   

  

 Recruiting Manager Kyle Chan, kyle.chan@hitachienergy.com will answer your questions about the position. Union representatives – Sveriges Ingenjörer: Nawzad Rashid, +46 107-38 91 48; Ledarna: Frank Hollstedt, +46 107-38 70 43; Unionen: Karin Ulvemark, +46 107-38 51 42 and Ingrid Rinaldo, +46 107-38 58 19. All other questions can be directed to Talent Acquisition Partner Fredrik Söder, fredrik.soder@hitachienergy.com.
   

  

 Hitachi Energy is a global technology leader in electrification, powering a sustainable energy future through innovative power grid technologies with digital at the core. Over three billion people depend on our technologies to power their daily lives.
   

  

 With over a century in pioneering mission-critical technologies like high-voltage, transformers, automation, and power electronics, we are addressing the most urgent energy challenge of our time – balancing soaring electricity demand, while decarbonizing the power system.
   

  

 Headquartered in Switzerland, we employ over 50,000 people in 60 countries and generate revenues of around $16 billion USD. We welcome you to apply today.

title: German Speaking GTM Marketing Manager
description:
**Tendium is looking for a
 *German speaking GTM (Go-to-Market) Marketing Manager* 
 to join our Marketing Team.**
**About Tendium**
 Tendium is the global pioneer in AI-driven public tender management, transforming how companies discover, analyse, and win government contracts. Our platform turns complex processes into something simple and transparent, empowering businesses to compete fairly for billions in public sector opportunities. We’ve been building our AI-native platform since 2018, leveraging a vast data moat, agentic AI, and tens of thousands of hours of R&D. Today, we serve large numbers of customers across Europe and are rapidly scaling.
   

  

 If you’re ready to make an impact and grow with us, we want to hear from you.
   

  

 The Opportunity
   

  

*In your role as GTM Marketing Manager, you will have a real ownership and significant impact on Tendium’s ability to scale in the DACH region from day one. You will be at the forefront of Tendium’s effort to establish ourselves in the DACH region. Your goal is to position our solutions in the market, drive new business, and build relationships. Your work will directly contribute to shaping Tendium’s products, services, and overall strategy.*
 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Launch GTM Strategy and the plan for how the product will be rolled out in DACH. Work closely with the commercial data team to identify entry points, messaging and what opportunities there are and continuously verify hypotheses by talking directly to customers and pilots.
* Build and maintain sustainable relationships with customers, industry experts, partners and key decision-makers across the public procurement sphere.
* Represent Tendium at trade fairs, conferences, and industry events, serving as an ambassador for our mission and solutions.
* Act as the "glue" and collaborate closely with the product teams, sales, customer success and the creative team in marketing to incorporate market feedback and continuously enhance the customer experience and guide product and marketing priorities
* Work with product teams to ensure the pricing and packaging model matches the customer's willingness to pay and market expectations.
* Utilize digital tools and CRM systems to analyze launch performance and key metrics such as lead conversion rates and CAC, continuously refining the strategy based on data and market feedback.
* Stay up to date on trends in public procurement, AI, and B2B SaaS, especially in the German speaking markets.


 What You Bring
   

  

* You have at least 6+ years of experience from similar roles.
* Native or fluent German, with excellent English communication skills.
* Experience with - or a solid understanding of - B2B SaaS environments.
* A commercial background within marketing, sales, or a related revenue-focused role.


 Nice-to-haves
   

  

* Good knowledge of the public procurement system in DACH & Europe.
* A completed degree in business administration or comparable education.


 Location
   

  

 This role is offered from our Stockholm office or on a fully remote basis, depending on preference.
   

  

 We are open to hiring someone working from Germany or Austria. If so, we will require you to visit the Stockholm HQ from time to time. The role will involve some travel as part of the work, regardless of being based in Sweden or elsewhere.
   

  

 Application
   

  

 We look forward to your application. We recruit on an ongoing basis, so apply as soon as possible. Successful candidates will initially be invited to an initial interview where you will have the opportunity to meet the team.
   

  

 To make this process even more efficient, feel free to sign in to your Connect profile and add your references at any stage.
   

  

 Please find additional information on our recruitment process
 *here.*
 If you want to know more about our culture and what you can expect when working at Tendium you can read more about it
 *here.*
 Equality and inclusion statemenet
   

  

 At Tendium, we don’t just value diversity, we celebrate it. We’re building a workplace where everyone should feel that they belong, where your voice is heard, and your experiences are respected. Different perspectives fuel our creativity and drive innovation, and we’re always looking for people who can bring something new to the team. Whoever you are, wherever you're from, we’d love to hear from you. Let’s shape the future together.
   

  

 At Tendium we’re using data to transform public procurement, giving our clients better access to public tenders and helping governments to curb waste and enhance transparency.
   

  

 For more information contact career@tendium.com

title: Network Security Architect
description:
På HiQ söker vi dig som brinner för nätverk och säkerhet och som vill kombinera arkitektur och hands-on-expertis. Hos oss får du både designa och bygga nästa generations nät- och säkerhetslösningar, från målarkitektur och design till implementation, optimering och vidareutveckling.
   

  

 Tänk dig att kliva in på jobbet och växla mellan att ta fram arkitektur och design för komplexa nätverk, och att själv vara nere i konfiguration, felsökning och optimering. Våra kunders miljöer spänner över stora datacenter, hybrida plattformar och molnlösningar med gott om utrymme för teknikdjup och innovation.
   

  

**Du kommer att…**
* Arbeta brett med switching, routing och wifi i både klassiska serverhallar och moderna moln- och hybridmiljöer. Du är med och bygger robusta lösningar med SD WAN, VPN och lastbalansering och ser till att de fungerar stabilt i vardagen, inte bara på ritbordet.
* Jobba med nätverkssäkerhet på både detaljnivå och helhetsnivå – från brandväggar, IDS/IPS, proxy, loggning och PKI till hur allt hänger ihop i en säker arkitektur. Samtidigt använder du programmering och automation för att förenkla, kvalitetssäkra och utveckla nätet.
